revision.text:
[[星まつり]]（ほしまつり）は仏教で災いを除くために個人個人の当年属星と本命星をまつる祭りのこと
七夕まつりも星祭と呼ばれているが内容は異なる
== 概要 == 密教の占星術では北斗七星の七つの星の内の一つを、その人の生まれ星として[[本命星]]と定めている。
そしてその人の運命を司る星と考えている。
また、一年ごとに巡ってくる運命を左右する星を「当年属星」と呼んでいる。
これらの星を供養し、個人の一年間の幸福を祈り、災いを除く祭りで一般に旧暦の年の初めに行われることが多い。
全国の様々な寺院で星まつりの行事が行われている。

== 概要 == 密教の占星術では北斗七星の七つの星の内の一つを、その人の生まれ星として[[本命星]]と定めている。
そしてその人の運命を司る星と考えている。
また、一年ごとに巡ってくる運命を左右する星を「当年属星」と呼んでいる。
これらの星を供養し、個人の一年間の幸福を祈り、災いを除く祭りで一般に旧暦の年の初めに行われることが多い。
全国の様々な寺院で星まつりの行事が行われている。 == 関連項目 == *[[阿含の星まつり]]

== 概要 == 密教の占星術では北斗七星の七つの星の内の一つを、その人の生まれ星として[[本命星]]と定めている。
そしてその人の運命を司る星と考えている。
また、一年ごとに巡ってくる運命を左右する星を「当年属星」と呼んでいる。
これらの星を供養し、個人の一年間の幸福を祈り、災いを除く祭りで一般に旧暦の年の初め（立春）に行われることが多い。
全国の様々な寺院で星まつりの行事が行われている。 == 関連項目 == *[[阿含の星まつり]]

'''星まつり'''（ほしまつり）は、[[仏教]]で災いを除くために個人個人の当年属星と本命星をまつる祭りのこと。 [[七夕]]まつりも星祭と呼ばれているが内容は異なる。
== 概要 == 密教の占星術では北斗七星の七つの星の内の一つを、その人の生まれ星として[[本命星]]と定めている。 そしてその人の運命を司る星と考えている。
また、一年ごとに巡ってくる運命を左右する星を「当年属星」と呼んでいる。 これらの星を供養し、個人の一年間の幸福を祈り、災いを除く祭りで一般に旧暦の年の初め（立春）に行われることが多い。 全国の様々な寺院で星まつりの行事が行われている。
== 関連項目 == * [[阿含の星まつり]]
[[Category:仏教|ほしまつり]]

== 概要 == 密教の占星術では北斗七星の七つの星の内の一つを、その人の生まれ星として[[本命星]]と定めている。 そしてその人の運命を司る星と考えている。
また、一年ごとに巡ってくる運命を左右する星を「当年属星」と呼んでいる。 これらの星を供養し、個人の一年間の幸福を祈り、災いを除く祭りで一般に旧暦の年の初め（立春）に行われることが多い。 全国の様々な寺院で星まつりの行事が行われている。

[[Category:密教|ほしまつり]] [[Category:仏教行事|ほしまつり]]

'''星まつり'''（ほしまつり）は、[[仏教]]（主に密教）で災いを除くために個人個人の当年星（とうねんじょう、当年属星とも）と[[本命星]]（ほんみょうじょう）をまつる祭りのこと。星供養（ほしくよう）ともいう。なお、[[七夕]]まつりも星祭と呼ばれているが内容は異なる。
== 概要 == 密教の占星術では、北斗七星の七つの星の内の一つをその人の生まれ星として本命星と定め、運命を司る星と考える。
また、一年ごとに巡ってくる運命を左右する星を「当年属星」と呼んでいる。これらの星を供養し、個人の一年間の幸福を祈り、災いを除く祭りで一般に旧暦の年の初め（立春）に行われることが多い。全国の様々な寺院で星まつり・星供の行事が行われている。

{{Buddhism}} '''星まつり'''（ほしまつり）は、[[仏教]]（主に密教）で災いを除くために個人個人の当年星（とうねんじょう、当年属星とも）と[[本命星]]（ほんみょうじょう）をまつる祭りのこと。星供養（ほしくよう）、星供（ほしく）ともいう。なお、[[七夕]]まつりも星祭と呼ばれているが内容は異なる。
== 概要 == 密教の占星術では、北斗七星の七つの星の内の一つをその人の生まれ星として本命星と定め、運命を司る星と考える。
また、一年ごとに巡ってくる運命を左右する星を「当年属星」と呼んでいる。これらの星を供養し、個人の一年間の幸福を祈り、災いを除く祭りで一般に旧暦の年の初め（立春）に行われることが多い。全国の様々な寺院で星まつり・星供の行事が行われている。

{{密教2}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ほしまつり}} [[Category:密教|ほしまつり]] [[Category:仏教行事|ほしまつり]]

{{Buddhism}} '''星まつり'''（ほしまつり）は、[[仏教]]（主に[[密教]]）で災いを除くために個人個人の当年星（とうねんじょう、当年属星とも）と[[本命星]]（ほんみょうじょう）を祀る修法と祭事を目的とした密教的なお祭りのこと。「'''星供養'''」（ほしくよう）、「'''星供'''」（ほしく）ともいう。なお、[[七夕]]まつりも星祭と呼ばれているが内容は異なる。
== 概要 == [[星曼荼羅]]に代表される密教占星法では、密教の修法と密教の天文占星を二本の柱として、北斗七星の七つの星の内の一つをその人の生まれ星として本命星と定め、運命を司る星と考える。
また、一年ごとに巡ってくる運命を左右する星を「当年属星」と呼んでいる。これらの星を供養し、個人の一年間の幸福を祈り、災いを除く祭りで一般に旧暦の年の初め（立春）に行われることが多い。全国の様々な寺院で星まつり・星供の行事が行われている。
== 脚注 == == 参考文献 == == 関連項目 == *[[星曼荼羅]] *[[宿曜道]] *[[文殊師利菩薩及諸仙所説吉凶時日善悪宿曜経|宿曜経]] *[[北斗七星]] *[[陰陽道]] *[[妙見菩薩]] *[[大日如来]] *[[阿含の星まつり]]
{{密教2}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ほしまつり}} [[Category:密教|ほしまつり]] [[Category:仏教行事|ほしまつり]]

{{Buddhism}} '''星まつり'''（ほしまつり）は、[[仏教]]の中で後期大乗の教えに属する[[密教]]において、「[[一字金輪仏頂]]」と呼ばれる大日金輪や釈迦金輪を主尊とする『[[星曼荼羅]]』や、[[妙見]]菩薩を主尊とする『[[妙見曼荼羅]]』を本尊として、天下国家や個人の災いを除くために、その年の主な星曜や各個人の当年星（とうねんじょう<ref>「当年星」は、別名を「当年属星」ともいう。</ref>）と、[[本命星]]（ほんみょうじょう）等を祀る修法と祭事を目的とした密教的なお祭りのこと。「'''星供養'''」（ほしくよう）、「'''星供'''」（ほしく）ともいう。なお、「[[七夕]]まつり」も星祭りと呼ばれているが内容は異なる。
== 概要 == 『[[星曼荼羅]]』に代表される密教占星法では、密教の修法と密教の天文占星を二本の柱として、北斗七星の七つの星の内の一つをその人の生まれ星として本命星と定め、運命を司る星と考える。
また、一年ごとに巡ってくる運命を左右する星を「当年属星」と呼んでいる。これらの星を供養し、個人の一年間の幸福を祈り、災いを除く祭りで一般に旧暦の年の初め（立春）に行われることが多い。全国の様々な寺院で星まつり・星供の行事が行われている。
== 脚注 == <references/> == 参考文献 == == 関連項目 == *[[星曼荼羅]] *[[宿曜道]] *[[文殊師利菩薩及諸仙所説吉凶時日善悪宿曜経|宿曜経]] *[[北斗七星]] *[[陰陽道]] *[[妙見菩薩]] *[[大日如来]] *[[阿含の星まつり]]
{{密教2}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ほしまつり}} [[Category:密教|ほしまつり]] [[Category:仏教行事|ほしまつり]]

{{Buddhism}} '''星まつり'''（ほしまつり）は、[[仏教]]の中で後期大乗の教えに属する[[密教]]において、「[[一字金輪仏頂]]」と呼ばれる[[大日金輪]]や[[釈迦金輪]]を主尊とする『[[星曼荼羅]]』や、[[妙見]]菩薩を主尊とする『[[妙見曼荼羅]]』を本尊として、天下国家や個人の災いを除くために、その年の主な星曜や各個人の当年星（とうねんじょう<ref>「当年星」は、別名を「当年属星」ともいう。</ref>）と、[[本命星]]（ほんみょうじょう）等を祀る修法と祭事を目的とした密教的なお祭りのこと。「'''星供養'''」（ほしくよう）、「'''星供'''」（ほしく）、あるいは「'''北斗法'''」ともいう。なお、「[[七夕]]まつり」も星祭りと呼ばれているが内容は異なる。
== 概要 == 『[[星曼荼羅]]』に代表される密教占星法では、密教の修法と密教の天文占星を二本の柱として、北斗七星の七つの星の内の一つをその人の生まれ星として本命星と定め、運命を司る星と考える。
また、一年ごとに巡ってくる運命を左右する星を「当年属星」と呼んでいる。これらの星を供養し、個人の一年間の幸福を祈り、災いを除く祭りで一般に旧暦の年の初め（立春）に行われることが多い。全国の様々な寺院で星まつり・星供の行事が行われている。
== 脚注 == <references/> == 参考文献 == *岩原諦信 著 『星供と其科学』、松本日進堂本店、昭和17年（1942年）刊。 * * *
== 関連項目 == *[[星曼荼羅]] *[[宿曜道]] *[[文殊師利菩薩及諸仙所説吉凶時日善悪宿曜経|宿曜経]] *[[北斗七星]] *[[陰陽道]] *[[妙見菩薩]] *[[大日如来]] *[[阿含の星まつり]]
{{密教2}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ほしまつり}} [[Category:密教|ほしまつり]] [[Category:仏教行事|ほしまつり]]

{{Buddhism}} '''星まつり'''（ほしまつり：星祭り）は、[[仏教]]の中で後期大乗の教えに属する[[密教]]において、「[[一字金輪仏頂]]」と呼ばれる[[大日金輪]]や[[釈迦金輪]]を主尊とする『[[星曼荼羅]]』や、七星[[如意輪観音]]を主尊とする『[[七星如意輪曼荼羅]]』や、[[妙見]]菩薩を主尊とする『[[妙見曼荼羅]]』を本尊として、天下国家に起こる各種の災害や、個人の災いを除くために、その年の主な星曜や各個人の当年星（とうねんじょう<ref>「当年星」は、別名を「当年属星」ともいう。</ref>）と、[[本命星]]（ほんみょうじょう）等を祀る修法と祭事を目的とした密教的なお祭りのこと。「'''星供養'''」（ほしくよう）、「'''星供'''」（ほしく）、あるいは「'''北斗法'''」ともいう。なお、「[[七夕]]まつり」も星祭りと呼ばれているが内容は異なる。
== 概要 == 『[[星曼荼羅]]』に代表される密教占星法では、密教の修法と密教の天文占星を二本の柱として、[[九曜]]星の九つの星や、[[北斗七星]]の七つの星の内の一つをその人の生まれ星として本命星と定め、運命を司る星と考える。
また、一年ごとに巡ってくる運命を左右する星を「当年属星」と呼んでいる。これらの星を供養し、個人の一年間の幸福を祈り、災いを除く祭りで一般に旧暦の年の初め（立春）に行われることが多い。全国の様々な寺院で星まつり・星供の行事が行われている。
== 脚注 == <references/> == 参考文献 == *[[岩原諦信]] 著 『星供と其科学』、松本日進堂本店、昭和17年（1942年）刊。 *[[矢野道雄]] 著 『密教占星術』 - 宿曜道とインド占星術 - 、東京美術、昭和61年（1986年）刊。 *[[林温]] 著 『妙見菩薩と星曼荼羅』（日本の美術№377）、[[文化庁]]・[[東京国立博物館]]・[[京都国立博物館]]・[[奈良国立博物館]] 監修、至文堂、1997年刊。 *
== 関連項目 == *[[星曼荼羅]] *[[宿曜道]] *[[文殊師利菩薩及諸仙所説吉凶時日善悪宿曜経|宿曜経]] *[[北斗七星]] *[[陰陽道]] *[[妙見菩薩]] *[[大日如来]] *[[阿含の星まつり]]
{{密教2}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ほしまつり}} [[Category:密教|ほしまつり]] [[Category:仏教行事|ほしまつり]]

{{Buddhism}} '''星まつり'''（ほしまつり：星祭り）は、[[仏教]]の中で後期大乗の教えに属する[[密教]]において、「[[一字金輪仏頂]]」と呼ばれる[[大日金輪]]や[[釈迦金輪]]を主尊とする『[[星曼荼羅]]』や、七星[[如意輪観音]]を主尊とする『[[七星如意輪曼荼羅]]』や、[[妙見]]菩薩を主尊とする『[[妙見曼荼羅]]』を本尊として、天下国家に起こる各種の災害や、個人の災いを除くために、その年の主な星曜や各個人の当年星（とうねんじょう<ref>「当年星」は、別名を「当年属星」ともいう。</ref>）と、[[本命星]]（ほんみょうじょう）等を祀る修法と祭事を目的とした密教的なお祭りのこと。「'''星供養'''」（ほしくよう）、「'''星供'''」（ほしく）、あるいは「'''北斗法'''」ともいう。本来は中国の[[道教]]の祭儀であり、インドの仏教とは無関係であったが、密教側が仏教的に脚色して採り入れたものである。なお、「[[七夕]]まつり」も星祭りと呼ばれているが内容は異なる。
== 概要 == 『[[星曼荼羅]]』に代表される密教占星法では、密教の修法と密教の天文占星を二本の柱として、[[九曜]]星の九つの星や、[[北斗七星]]の七つの星の内の一つをその人の生まれ星として本命星と定め、運命を司る星と考える。
また、一年ごとに巡ってくる運命を左右する星を「当年属星」と呼んでいる。これらの星を供養し、個人の一年間の幸福を祈り、災いを除く祭りで一般に旧暦の年の初め（立春）に行われることが多い。全国の様々な寺院で星まつり・星供の行事が行われている。
== 脚注 == <references/> == 参考文献 == *[[岩原諦信]] 著 『星供と其科学』、松本日進堂本店、昭和17年（1942年）刊。 *[[矢野道雄]] 著 『密教占星術』 - 宿曜道とインド占星術 - 、東京美術、昭和61年（1986年）刊。 *[[林温]] 著 『妙見菩薩と星曼荼羅』（日本の美術№377）、[[文化庁]]・[[東京国立博物館]]・[[京都国立博物館]]・[[奈良国立博物館]] 監修、至文堂、1997年刊。 *
== 関連項目 == *[[星曼荼羅]] *[[宿曜道]] *[[文殊師利菩薩及諸仙所説吉凶時日善悪宿曜経|宿曜経]] *[[北斗七星]] *[[陰陽道]] *[[妙見菩薩]] *[[大日如来]] *[[阿含の星まつり]]
{{密教2}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ほしまつり}} [[Category:密教|ほしまつり]] [[Category:仏教行事|ほしまつり]]

{{Buddhism}} '''星まつり'''（ほしまつり：星祭り）<ref>「[[七夕]]まつり」も星祭りと呼ばれているが内容は異なる。</ref>は、[[仏教]]の中で後期大乗の教えに属する[[密教]]において、「[[一字金輪仏頂]]」と呼ばれる[[大日金輪]]や[[釈迦金輪]]を主尊とする『[[星曼荼羅]]』や、七星[[如意輪観音]]を主尊とする『[[七星如意輪曼荼羅]]』や、[[妙見]]菩薩を主尊とする『[[妙見曼荼羅]]』を本尊として、天下国家に起こる各種の災害や、個人の災いを除くために、その年の主な星曜や各個人の当年星（とうねんじょう）<ref>「当年星」は、別名を「当年属星」ともいう。</ref>と、本命星（ほんみょうじょう）<ref>[[九星気学]]でも本命星という用語を使うが九星とは異なり、[[北斗七星]]や[[九曜]]である。</ref>等を祀る修法と祭事を目的とした密教的なお祭りのこと。「'''星供養'''」（ほしくよう）、「'''星供'''」（ほしく）、あるいは「'''北斗法'''」ともいう。本来は中国の[[道教]]の祭儀であり、インドの仏教とは無関係であったが、密教側が仏教的に脚色して採り入れたものである。
== 概要 == 『[[星曼荼羅]]』に代表される密教占星法では、密教の修法と密教の天文占星を二本の柱として、[[九曜]]星の九つの星や、[[北斗七星]]の七つの星の内の一つをその人の生まれ星として本命星と定め、運命を司る星と考える。
また、一年ごとに巡ってくる運命を左右する星を「当年属星」と呼んでいる。これらの星を供養し、個人の一年間の幸福を祈り、災いを除く祭りで一般に旧暦の年の初め（立春）や冬至に行われる。全国の様々な寺院で星まつり・星供の行事が行われている。
== 脚注 == <references/> == 参考文献 == *[[岩原諦信]] 著 『星供と其科学』、松本日進堂本店、昭和17年（1942年）刊。 *[[矢野道雄]] 著 『密教占星術』 - 宿曜道とインド占星術 - 、東京美術、昭和61年（1986年）刊。 *[[林温]] 著 『妙見菩薩と星曼荼羅』（日本の美術№377）、[[文化庁]]・[[東京国立博物館]]・[[京都国立博物館]]・[[奈良国立博物館]] 監修、至文堂、1997年刊。 *
== 関連項目 == *[[星曼荼羅]] *[[宿曜道]] *[[文殊師利菩薩及諸仙所説吉凶時日善悪宿曜経|宿曜経]] *[[北斗七星]] *[[陰陽道]] *[[妙見菩薩]] *[[大日如来]] *[[阿含の星まつり]]
{{密教2}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ほしまつり}} [[Category:密教|ほしまつり]] [[Category:仏教行事|ほしまつり]]

また、一年ごとに巡ってくる運命を左右する星を「当年属星」と呼んでいる。これらの星を供養し、個人の一年間の幸福を祈り、災いを除く祭りで一般に旧暦の元旦や、立春、冬至などに行われる。全国の様々な寺院で星まつり・星供の行事が行われている。

'''星祭'''（ほしまつり） #「[[七夕]]」の別名。詳細は該当項参照。 #「星供養」の俗称。本項ではこちらを解説する。 ----------- '''星まつり'''（ほしまつり：星祭り）は、[[仏教]]の中で後期大乗の教えに属する[[密教]]において、「[[一字金輪仏頂]]」と呼ばれる[[大日金輪]]や[[釈迦金輪]]を主尊とする『[[星曼荼羅]]』や、七星[[如意輪観音]]を主尊とする『[[七星如意輪曼荼羅]]』や、[[妙見]]菩薩を主尊とする『[[妙見曼荼羅]]』を本尊として、天下国家に起こる各種の災害や、個人の災いを除くために、その年の主な星曜や各個人の当年星（とうねんじょう）<ref>「当年星」は、別名を「当年属星」ともいう。</ref>と、本命星（ほんみょうじょう）<ref>[[九星気学]]でも本命星という用語を使うが九星とは異なり、こちらは[[北斗七星]]や[[九曜星]]のことである。</ref>等を祀る修法と祭事を目的とした密教的なお祭りのこと。「'''星供養'''」（ほしくよう）、「'''星供'''」（ほしく）、あるいは「'''北斗法'''」ともいう。本来は中国の[[道教]]の祭儀であり、インドの仏教とは無関係であったが、密教側が仏教的に脚色して採り入れたものである。
== 概要 == 『[[星曼荼羅]]』に代表される密教占星法では、密教の修法と密教の天文占星を二本の柱として、[[九曜]]星の九つの星や、[[北斗七星]]の七つの星の内の一つをその人の生まれ星として本命星と定め、運命を司る星と考える。
また、一年ごとに巡ってくる運命を左右する星を「当年属星」と呼んでいる。これらの星を供養し、個人の一年間の幸福を祈り、災いを除く祭りで一般に旧暦の元旦や、立春、冬至などに行われる。全国の様々な寺院で星まつり・星供の行事が行われている。

'''星祭'''（ほしまつり） #「[[七夕]]」の別名。詳細は該当項参照。 #「星供養」の俗称。本項ではこちらを解説する。 ----------- '''星まつり'''（ほしまつり：星祭り）は、旧暦の元旦や、立春、冬至などに<ref>道教の段階では冬至に行うのが本来の姿で、立春や旧暦元旦とは関係がなかった。仏教化してから後には立春や旧暦元旦にも行うようになった。</ref>行われる[[仏教]]の儀式で、天下国家に起こる各種の災害や個人の災いを除くものである。「'''星供養'''」（ほしくよう）、「'''星供'''」（ほしく）、あるいは「'''北斗法'''」ともいう。

『[[星曼荼羅]]』に代表される密教占星法では、密教の修法と密教の天文占星を二本の柱として、[[九曜]]星の九つの星や、[[北斗七星]]の七つの星の内の一つをその人の生まれ星として本命星と定め、運命を司る星と考える。
また、一年ごとに巡ってくる運命を左右する星を「当年属星」と呼んでいる。これらの星を供養し、個人の一年間の幸福を祈り、災いを除く。全国の様々な寺院で星まつり・星供の行事が行われている。

'''星祭'''（ほしまつり） #「[[七夕]]」の別名。詳細は該当項参照。 #「星供養」の俗称。本項ではこちらを解説する。 ----------- '''星まつり'''（ほしまつり、星祭り）は、旧暦の元旦や、立春、冬至などに<ref>道教の段階では冬至に行うのが本来の姿で、立春や旧暦元旦とは関係がなかった。仏教化してから後には立春や旧暦元旦にも行うようになった。</ref>行われる[[仏教]]の儀式で、天下国家に起こる各種の災害や個人の災いを除くものである。「'''星供養'''」（ほしくよう）、「'''星供'''」（ほしく）、あるいは「'''北斗法'''」ともいう。
== 概要 == 仏教の中で後期大乗の教えに属する[[密教]]において、「[[一字金輪仏頂]]」と呼ばれる[[大日金輪]]や[[釈迦金輪]]を主尊とする『[[星曼荼羅]]』や、七星[[如意輪観音]]を主尊とする『[[七星如意輪曼荼羅]]』や、[[妙見菩薩]]を主尊とする『[[妙見曼荼羅]]』を本尊として、その年の主な星曜や各個人の当年星（とうねんじょう）<ref>「当年星」は、別名を「当年属星」ともいう。</ref>と、本命星（ほんみょうじょう）<ref>[[九星気学]]でも本命星という用語を使うが九星とは異なり、こちらは[[北斗七星]]や[[九曜星]]のことである。</ref>等を祀る修法と祭事を目的とした密教の祭儀である。本来は中国の[[道教]]の[[冬至]]の祭儀であり、[[インドの仏教]]とは無関係であったが、密教側が道教から採り入れ仏教的に脚色したものである。
『[[星曼荼羅]]』に代表される密教占星法では、密教の修法と密教の天文占星を二本の柱として、[[九曜]]星の九つの星や、[[北斗七星]]の七つの星の内の一つをその人の生まれ星として本命星と定め、運命を司る星と考える。
また、一年ごとに巡ってくる運命を左右する星を「当年属星」と呼んでいる。これらの星を供養し、個人の一年間の幸福を祈り、災いを除く。全国の様々な寺院で星まつり・星供の行事が行われている。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *[[岩原諦信]] 著 『星供と其科学』、松本日進堂本店、昭和17年（1942年）刊。 *[[矢野道雄]] 著 『密教占星術』 - 宿曜道とインド占星術 - 、東京美術、昭和61年（1986年）刊。 *[[林温]] 著 『妙見菩薩と星曼荼羅』（日本の美術№377）、[[文化庁]]・[[東京国立博物館]]・[[京都国立博物館]]・[[奈良国立博物館]] 監修、至文堂、1997年刊。 *武田和昭 『星曼荼羅の研究』 法藏館、1995年
== 関連項目 == *[[宿曜道]] *[[文殊師利菩薩及諸仙所説吉凶時日善悪宿曜経]] *[[北斗七星]] *[[陰陽道]] *[[妙見菩薩]] *[[大日如来]] *[[阿含の星まつり]]
{{密教2}} {{Buddhism2}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ほしまつり}} [[Category:密教]] [[Category:仏教行事]] [[Category:陰陽道]] [[Category:神仏習合]] [[Category:宿曜道]]

また、一年ごとに巡ってくる運命を左右する星を「当年属星」と呼んでいる。これらの星を供養し、個人の一年間の幸福を祈り、災いを除く。全国の様々な寺院で星まつり・星供の行事が行われている。

== 参考文献 == *[[岩原諦信]] 著 『星供と其科学』、松本日進堂本店、昭和17年（1942年）刊。 *[[矢野道雄]] 著 『密教占星術』 - 宿曜道とインド占星術 - 、東京美術、昭和61年（1986年）刊。 *[[林温]] 著 『妙見菩薩と星曼荼羅』（日本の美術№377）、[[文化庁]]・[[東京国立博物館]]・[[京都国立博物館]]・[[奈良国立博物館]] 監修、至文堂、1997年刊。 *武田和昭 『星曼荼羅の研究』 法藏館、1995年

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Cite_sources|date=2019-10-07}} {{nihongo|Shukuyōdō|宿曜道}} is a type of {{ill|astrology|ja|占星術}} introduced to {{ill|Japan|ja|日本}} during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} by monks studying abroad, including {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, as a branch of {{ill|Esoteric Buddhism|ja|密教}}. It is also known as "Esoteric astrology" or "Shukuyō astrology." == Overview == The practice blends elements of {{ill|Indian astrology|ja|インド占星術}} (..."

revision.text:
{{Cite_sources|date=2019-10-07}}
{{nihongo|Shukuyōdō|宿曜道}} is a ty{{ill|Star Festival|ja|星まつり}}pe of {{ill|astrology|ja|占星術}} introduced to {{ill|Japan|ja|日本}} during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} by monks studying abroad, including {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, as a branch of {{ill|Esoteric Buddhism|ja|密教}}. It is also known as "Esoteric astrology" or "Shukuyō astrology."
== Overview ==
The practice blends elements of {{ill|Indian astrology|ja|インド占星術}} (a fusion of {{ill|Western astrology|ja|西洋占星術}} and ancient Indian lunar astrology), Taoist celestial deity worship, and {{ill|Yin-Yang and the Five Phases|ja|陰陽五行説}}. It determines auspicious and inauspicious days based on the movements of celestial bodies such as the {{ill|Big Dipper|ja|北斗七星}}, {{ill|Navagraha|ja|九曜}}, {{ill|Zodiac|ja|十二宮}}, and lunar mansions like the {{ill|Nakshatra|ja|二十七宿}} or {{ill|Twenty-Eight Mansions|ja|二十八宿|Twenty-Eight Mansions=Twenty-Eight Mansions|lt=Lunar mansions}}. When an inauspicious day is identified, practitioners would perform rituals to honor the deities associated with those stars to improve fortune.
The foundational scriptures include the {{ill|Sutra of Sukuyokyo|ja|宿曜経}}, {{ill|Brahma Agni Sutra|ja|梵天火羅九曜}}, and {{ill|Shichi Yō Seishin Betsu Gōhō|ja|七曜星辰別行法}}. One unique technique is {{ill|Sankū Hikkyū|ja|三九秘宿}}, which has a simplified version popularized as "Shukuyō astrology."
In {{ill|Esoteric Buddhism|ja|密教}}, selecting auspicious dates for rituals such as statue construction, ceremonies, or consecration was a crucial task. The {{ill|Mahāvairocanābhisaṃbodhi Sūtra Commentary|ja|大日経疏}} by {{ill|Ichigyō|ja|一行}} emphasized the importance of this process as a test of an {{ill|Acharya|ja|阿闍梨}}'s abilities.
Monks such as {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, {{ill|Ennin|ja|円仁}}, and {{ill|Enchin|ja|円珍}} brought the {{ill|Sutra of Sukuyokyo|ja|宿曜経}} to Japan, and {{ill|Ninkan|ja|仁観}} further developed its study. In {{ill|957|ja|957}} ({{ill|Tento era|ja|天徳}} 1), {{ill|Nichien|ja|日延}} brought the {{ill|Futen Calendar|ja|符天暦}} from {{ill|Wuyue|ja|呉越}}, intensifying research. {{ill|Hōzō (Japanese monk)|ja|法蔵}} in {{ill|Ōwa era|ja|応和}} 1 ({{ill|963|ja|963}}) debated {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}} over the use of auspicious dates for {{ill|Emperor Murakami|ja|村上天皇}}’s offerings. This marked the formal establishment of Shukuyōdō in Japan.
== Legacy ==
* In the {{ill|Genji Monogatari|ja|源氏物語}}, a scene in the "Kiritsubo" chapter describes {{ill|Hikaru Genji|ja|光源氏}}’s fate being predicted by a Shukuyō astrologer. * During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoritsune|ja|藤原頼経}} employed a Shukuyō practitioner, {{ill|Chinhyo|ja|珍誉}}, to select auspicious locations for the {{ill|Shogunate|ja|御所}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Shukuyō astrology|ja|宿曜占星術}} * {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} * {{ill|Futen Calendar|ja|符天暦}} * {{ill|Rokumei|ja|禄命}} *
== External Links == * {{kotobank|2=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|宿曜道}} * [https://yakumoin.net/宿曜占星術 宿曜占星術 八雲院] * [https://fs-astrology-members.com/宿曜占星術 宿曜占星術+news] * [https://syukuyo.com/宿曜秘宝協会 宿曜秘宝協会] * [https://kosei-do.co.jp/fortune/uranai.php 宿曜占星術 光晴堂] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Sukuyōdō]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sukuyōdō]][[Category:Shukuyōdō]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Sukuyōdō]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sukuyōdō]][[Category:Astrology]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Sukuyōdō]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sukuyōdō]][[Category:Days of the week]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Sukuyōdō]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sukuyōdō]][[Category:Esoteric Buddhism]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Sukuyōdō]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sukuyōdō]][[Category:History of astronomy in Japan]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Sukuyōdō]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sukuyōdō]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Sukuyōdō]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sukuyōdō]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Sukuyōdō]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sukuyōdō]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[category{Sukuyōdō]] [[ja:宿曜道]] [[Category:Shukuyōdō forks]]

{{Cite_sources|date=2019-10-07]]
{{nihongo|Shukuyōdō|宿曜道]] is a ty{{ill|Star Festival|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:星まつり]]pe of {{ill|astrology|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:占星術]] introduced to {{ill|Japan|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:日本]] during the {{ill|Heian period|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:平安時代]] by monks studying abroad, including {{ill|Kūkai|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:空海]], as a branch of {{ill|Esoteric Buddhism|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:密教]]. It is also known as "Esoteric astrology" or "Shukuyō astrology."
== Overview ==
The practice blends elements of {{ill|Indian astrology|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:インド占星術]] (a fusion of {{ill|Western astrology|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:西洋占星術]] and ancient Indian lunar astrology), Taoist celestial deity worship, and {{ill|Yin-Yang and the Five Phases|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:陰陽五行説]]. It determines auspicious and inauspicious days based on the movements of celestial bodies such as the {{ill|Big Dipper|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:北斗七星]], {{ill|Navagraha|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:九曜]], {{ill|Zodiac|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:十二宮]], and lunar mansions like the {{ill|Nakshatra|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:二十七宿]] or {{ill|Twenty-Eight Mansions|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:二十八宿|Twenty-Eight Mansions=Twenty-Eight Mansions|lt=Lunar mansions]]. When an inauspicious day is identified, practitioners would perform rituals to honor the deities associated with those stars to improve fortune.
The foundational scriptures include the {{ill|Sutra of Sukuyokyo|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:宿曜経]], {{ill|Brahma Agni Sutra|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:梵天火羅九曜]], and {{ill|Shichi Yō Seishin Betsu Gōhō|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:七曜星辰別行法]]. One unique technique is {{ill|Sankū Hikkyū|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:三九秘宿]], which has a simplified version popularized as "Shukuyō astrology."
In {{ill|Esoteric Buddhism|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:密教]], selecting auspicious dates for rituals such as statue construction, ceremonies, or consecration was a crucial task. The {{ill|Mahāvairocanābhisaṃbodhi Sūtra Commentary|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:大日経疏]] by {{ill|Ichigyō|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:一行]] emphasized the importance of this process as a test of an {{ill|Acharya|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:阿闍梨]]'s abilities.
Monks such as {{ill|Kūkai|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:空海]], {{ill|Ennin|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:円仁]], and {{ill|Enchin|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:円珍]] brought the {{ill|Sutra of Sukuyokyo|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:宿曜経]] to Japan, and {{ill|Ninkan|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:仁観]] further developed its study. In {{ill|957|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:957]] ({{ill|Tento era|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:天徳]] 1), {{ill|Nichien|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:日延]] brought the {{ill|Futen Calendar|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:符天暦]] from {{ill|Wuyue|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:呉越]], intensifying research. {{ill|Hōzō (Japanese monk)|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:法蔵]] in {{ill|Ōwa era|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:応和]] 1 ({{ill|963|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:963]]) debated {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:賀茂保憲]] over the use of auspicious dates for {{ill|Emperor Murakami|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:村上天皇]]’s offerings. This marked the formal establishment of Shukuyōdō in Japan.
== Legacy ==
* In the {{ill|Genji Monogatari|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:源氏物語]], a scene in the "Kiritsubo" chapter describes {{ill|Hikaru Genji|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:光源氏]]’s fate being predicted by a Shukuyō astrologer. * During the {{ill|Kamakura period|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:鎌倉時代]], {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoritsune|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:藤原頼経]] employed a Shukuyō practitioner, {{ill|Chinhyo|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:珍誉]], to select auspicious locations for the {{ill|Shogunate|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:御所]].

Monks such as {{ill|Kūkai|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:空海]], {{ill|Ennin|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:円仁]], and {{ill|Enchin|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:円珍]] brought the {{ill|Sutra of Sukuyokyo|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:宿曜経]] to Japan, and {{ill|Ninkan|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:仁観]] further developed its study. In {{ill|957|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:957]] ({{ill|Tento era|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:天徳]] 1), {{ill|Nichien|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:日延]] brought the {{ill|Futen Calendar|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:符天暦]] from {{ill|Wuyue|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:呉越]], intensifying research. {{ill|Hōzō (Japanese monk)|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:法蔵]] in {{ill|Ōwa era|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:応和]] 1 ({{ill|963|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:963]]) debated {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:賀茂保憲]] over the use of auspicious dates for {{ill|Emperor Murakami|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:村上天皇]]’s offerings. This marked the formal establishment of Shukuyōdō in Japan.
== Legacy ==
* In the {{ill|Genji Monogatari|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:源氏物語]], a scene in the "Kiritsubo" chapter describes {{ill|Hikaru Genji|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:光源氏]]’s fate being predicted by a Shukuyō astrologer. * During the {{ill|Kamakura period|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:鎌倉時代]], {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoritsune|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:藤原頼経]] employed a Shukuyō practitioner, {{ill|Chinhyo|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:珍誉]], to select auspicious locations for the {{ill|Shogunate|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:御所]].

{{Cite_sources|date=2019-10-07]]
{{nihongo|Shukuyōdō|宿曜道]] is a ty{{ill|Star Festival|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:星まつり]]pe of {{ill|astrology|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:占星術]] introduced to {{ill|Japan|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:日本]] during the {{ill|Heian period|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:平安時代]] by monks studying abroad, including {{ill|Kūkai|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:空海]], as a branch of {{ill|Esoteric Buddhism|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:密教]]. It is also known as "Esoteric astrology" or "Shukuyō astrology."
== Overview ==
The practice blends elements of {{ill|Indian astrology|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:インド占星術]] (a fusion of {{ill|Western astrology|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:西洋占星術]] and ancient Indian lunar astrology), Taoist celestial deity worship, and {{ill|Yin-Yang and the Five Phases|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:陰陽五行説]]. It determines auspicious and inauspicious days based on the movements of celestial bodies such as the {{ill|Big Dipper|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:北斗七星]], {{ill|Navagraha|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:九曜]], {{ill|Zodiac|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:十二宮]], and lunar mansions like the {{ill|Nakshatra|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:二十七宿]] or {{ill|Twenty-Eight Mansions|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:二十八宿|Twenty-Eight Mansions=Twenty-Eight Mansions|lt=Lunar mansions]]. When an inauspicious day is identified, practitioners would perform rituals to honor the deities associated with those stars to improve fortune.
The foundational scriptures include the {{ill|Sutra of Sukuyokyo|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:宿曜経]], {{ill|Brahma Agni Sutra|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:梵天火羅九曜]], and {{ill|Shichi Yō Seishin Betsu Gōhō|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:七曜星辰別行法]]. One unique technique is {{ill|Sankū Hikkyū|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:三九秘宿]], which has a simplified version popularized as "Shukuyō astrology."
In {{ill|Esoteric Buddhism|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:密教]], selecting auspicious dates for rituals such as statue construction, ceremonies, or consecration was a crucial task. The {{ill|Mahāvairocanābhisaṃbodhi Sūtra Commentary|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:大日経疏]] by {{ill|Ichigyō|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:一行]] emphasized the importance of this process as a test of an {{ill|Acharya|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:阿闍梨]]'s abilities.
Monks such as {{ill|Kūkai|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:空海]], {{ill|Ennin|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:円仁]], and {{ill|Enchin|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:円珍]] brought the {{ill|Sutra of Sukuyokyo|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:宿曜経]] to Japan, and {{ill|Ninkan|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:仁観]] further developed its study. In {{ill|957|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:957]] ({{ill|Tento era|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:天徳]] 1), {{ill|Nichien|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:日延]] brought the {{ill|Futen Calendar|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:符天暦]] from {{ill|Wuyue|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:呉越]], intensifying research. {{ill|Hōzō (Japanese monk)|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:法蔵]] in {{ill|Ōwa era|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:応和]] 1 ({{ill|963|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:963]]) debated {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:賀茂保憲]] over the use of auspicious dates for {{ill|Emperor Murakami|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:村上天皇]]’s offerings. This marked the formal establishment of Shukuyōdō in Japan.
== Legacy ==
* In the {{ill|Genji Monogatari|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:源氏物語]], a scene in the "Kiritsubo" chapter describes {{ill|Hikaru Genji|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:光源氏]]’s fate being predicted by a Shukuyō astrologer. * During the {{ill|Kamakura period|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:鎌倉時代]], {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoritsune|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:藤原頼経]] employed a Shukuyō practitioner, {{ill|Chinhyo|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:珍誉]], to select auspicious locations for the {{ill|Shogunate|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:御所]].

In {{ill|Esoteric Buddhism|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:密教]], selecting auspicious dates for rituals such as statue construction, ceremonies, or consecration was a crucial task. The {{ill|Mahāvairocanābhisaṃbodhi Sūtra Commentary|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:大日経疏]] by {{ill|Ichigyō|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:一行]] emphasized the importance of this process as a test of an {{ill|Acharya|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:阿闍梨]]'s abilities.
Monks such as {{ill|Kūkai|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:空海]], {{ill|Ennin|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:円仁]], and {{ill|Enchin|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:円珍]] brought the {{ill|Sutra of Sukuyokyo|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:宿曜経]] to Japan, and {{ill|Ninkan|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:仁観]] further developed its study. In {{ill|957|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:957]] ({{ill|Tento era|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:天徳]] 1), {{ill|Nichien|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:日延]] brought the {{ill|Futen Calendar|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:符天暦]] from {{ill|Wuyue|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:呉越]], intensifying research. {{ill|Hōzō (Japanese monk)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:法蔵]] in {{ill|Ōwa era|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:応和]] 1 ({{ill|963|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:963]]) debated {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:賀茂保憲]] over the use of auspicious dates for {{ill|Emperor Murakami|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:村上天皇]]’s offerings. This marked the formal establishment of Shukuyōdō in Japan.
== Legacy ==
* In the {{ill|Genji Monogatari|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:源氏物語]], a scene in the "Kiritsubo" chapter describes {{ill|Hikaru Genji|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:光源氏]]’s fate being predicted by a Shukuyō astrologer. * During the {{ill|Kamakura period|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:鎌倉時代]], {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoritsune|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:藤原頼経]] employed a Shukuyō practitioner, {{ill|Chinhyo|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:珍誉]], to select auspicious locations for the {{ill|Shogunate|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:御所]].
== References == {{reflist]]
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Shukuyō astrology|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:宿曜占星術]] * {{ill|Onmyōdō|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:陰陽道]] * {{ill|Futen Calendar|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:符天暦]] * {{ill|Rokumei|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:禄命]] *
== External Links == * {{kotobank|2=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|宿曜道]] * [https://yakumoin.net/宿曜占星術 宿曜占星術 八雲院] * [https://fs-astrology-members.com/宿曜占星術 宿曜占星術+news] * [https://syukuyo.com/宿曜秘宝協会 宿曜秘宝協会] * [https://kosei-do.co.jp/fortune/uranai.php 宿曜占星術 光晴堂] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shukuyōdō]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Astrology]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Days of the week]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Esoteric Buddhism]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:History of astronomy in Japan]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[ja:宿曜道]] [[Category:Shukuyōdō forks]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Star Festival]][[Category:qq]]

revision.text:
'''星祭'''（ほしまつり） #「[[七夕]]」の別名。詳細は該当項参照。 #「星供養」の俗称。本項ではこちらを解説する。 ----------- '''星まつり'''（ほしまつり、星祭り）は、旧暦の元旦や、立春、冬至などに<ref>道教の段階では冬至に行うのが本来の姿で、立春や旧暦元旦とは関係がなかった。仏教化してから後には立春や旧暦元旦にも行うようになった。</ref>行われる[[仏教]]の儀式で、天下国家に起こる各種の災害や個人の災いを除くものである。「'''星供養'''」（ほしくよう）、「'''星供'''」（ほしく）、あるいは「'''北斗法'''」ともいう。
== 概要 == [[File:Star Mandala (Horyuji).jpg|thumb|星曼荼羅([[法隆寺]]・蔵)]] 仏教の中で後期大乗の教えに属する[[密教]]において、「[[一字金輪仏頂]]」と呼ばれる[[大日金輪]]や[[釈迦金輪]]を主尊とする『[[星曼荼羅]]』や、七星[[如意輪観音]]を主尊とする『[[七星如意輪曼荼羅]]』や、[[妙見菩薩]]を主尊とする『[[妙見曼荼羅]]』を本尊として、その年の主な星曜や各個人の当年星（とうねんじょう）<ref>「当年星」は、別名を「当年属星」ともいう。</ref>と、本命星（ほんみょうじょう）<ref>[[九星気学]]でも本命星という用語を使うが九星とは異なり、こちらは[[北斗七星]]や[[九曜星]]のことである。</ref>等を祀る修法と祭事を目的とした密教の祭儀である。本来は中国の[[道教]]の[[冬至]]の祭儀であり、[[インドの仏教]]とは無関係であったが、密教側が道教から採り入れ仏教的に脚色したものである。
『[[星曼荼羅]]』に代表される密教占星法では、密教の修法と密教の天文占星を二本の柱として、[[九曜]]星の九つの星や、[[北斗七星]]の七つの星の内の一つをその人の生まれ星として本命星と定め、運命を司る星と考える。
また、一年ごとに巡ってくる運命を左右する星を「当年属星」と呼んでいる。これらの星を供養し、個人の一年間の幸福を祈り、災いを除く。全国の様々な寺院で星まつり・星供の行事が行われている。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *[[岩原諦信]] 著 『星供と其科学』、松本日進堂本店、昭和17年（1942年）刊。 *[[矢野道雄]] 著 『密教占星術』 - 宿曜道とインド占星術 - 、東京美術、昭和61年（1986年）刊。 *[[林温]] 著 『妙見菩薩と星曼荼羅』（日本の美術№377）、[[文化庁]]・[[東京国立博物館]]・[[京都国立博物館]]・[[奈良国立博物館]] 監修、至文堂、1997年刊。 *武田和昭 『星曼荼羅の研究』 法藏館、1995年
== 関連項目 == *[[宿曜道]] *[[文殊師利菩薩及諸仙所説吉凶時日善悪宿曜経]] *[[北斗七星]] *[[陰陽道]] *[[妙見菩薩]] *[[大日如来]] *[[阿含の星まつり]] *[[後七日御修法]]
{{密教2}} {{Buddhism2}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ほしまつり}} [[Category:密教]] [[Category:仏教行事]] [[Category:陰陽道]] [[Category:神仏習合]] [[Category:宿曜道]]

== 参考文献 == *[[岩原諦信]] 著 『星供と其科学』、松本日進堂本店、昭和17年（1942年）刊。 *[[矢野道雄]] 著 『密教占星術』 - 宿曜道とインド占星術 - 、東京美術、昭和61年（1986年）刊。 *[[林温]] 著 『妙見菩薩と星曼荼羅』（日本の美術№377）、[[文化庁]]・[[東京国立博物館]]・[[京都国立博物館]]・[[奈良国立博物館]] 監修、至文堂、1997年刊。 *武田和昭 『星曼荼羅の研究』 法藏館、1995年

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Add wikidata]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:29, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Add wikidata]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Hoshi Matsuri''' (星祭)
# An alternative name for [[:en:Qixi Festival|Tanabata]]. For details, see the corresponding article. # A colloquial term for "Star Memorial Service" (星供養). This article focuses on this topic.
== Overview ==
[[File:Star Mandala (Horyuji).jpg|thumb|Star Mandala ([[:en:Hōryū-ji|Hōryū-ji]] Collection)]]
'''Hoshi Matsuri''' (星まつり or 星祭り) is a [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhist]] ritual held on occasions such as the Lunar New Year, [[:en:Lichun|Risshun]], and [[:en:Winter solstice|Winter Solstice]].<ref>In Taoism, the ritual was originally conducted on the Winter Solstice and had no connection to Risshun or the Lunar New Year. It was only after being incorporated into Buddhism that it began to be observed on these additional dates.</ref> The purpose of this ritual is to ward off various disasters affecting both the state and individuals. It is also known as "'''Star Memorial Service'''” (星供養, Hoshikuyō), "'''Star Offering'''” (星供, Hoshiku), or the "'''Big Dipper Ritual'''” (北斗法, Hokutohō).
In [[:en:Mikkyō|Esoteric Buddhism]], which belongs to the later teachings of the {{ill|Mahayana|ja|大乗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mahayana|qq=|1=Mahayana|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18989147}} tradition, deities such as {{ill|Ichiji Kinrin Butchō|ja|一字金輪仏頂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiji Kinrin Butchō|qq=|1=Ekākṣaroṣṇīṣacakra|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352518}} (also known as {{ill|Dainichi Kinrin|ja|大日金輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainichi Kinrin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Shaka Kinrin|ja|釈迦金輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaka Kinrin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) are enshrined in mandalas like the {{ill|Star Mandala|ja|星曼荼羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star Mandala|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Other examples include the {{ill|Seven-Star Nyoirin Mandala|ja|七星如意輪曼荼羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven-Star Nyoirin Mandala|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} with [[:en:Cintāmaṇicakra|Nyoirin Kannon]] as the principal deity and the {{ill|Myōken Mandala|ja|妙見曼荼羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Mandala|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} featuring [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]]. These mandalas are central to rituals and ceremonies that honor the year's guiding star (当年星, tōnenjō)<ref>"Tōnenjō" is also referred to as "Tōnen Zokusei" (当年属星).</ref> and the natal star (本命星, honmyōjō).<ref>Although the term "honmyōsei" is also used in [[:en:Flying Star Feng Shui|Nine Star Ki]], in this context it refers to stars from the [[:en:Big Dipper|Big Dipper]] and the {{ill|Nine Luminaries|ja|九曜星|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nine Luminaries|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九曜|1=Navagraha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1078632}}, rather than the nine stars of Ki astrology.</ref>
Originally, this ritual was part of the [[:en:Taoism|Taoist]] Winter Solstice festival and had no relation to {{ill|Indian Buddhism|ja|インドの仏教|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Indian Buddhism|qq=|1=Buddhism in India|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2348029}}. However, it was later incorporated into Esoteric Buddhism, where it was adapted and reinterpreted within a Buddhist framework.
In Esoteric astrology, as represented by the Star Mandala, both esoteric rituals and celestial astrology form the core principles. One of the nine [[:en:Navagraha|Navagraha]] stars or the seven stars of the Big Dipper is assigned as a person's natal star (本命星), believed to govern their destiny.
The star that influences a person's fortune for the year is called the "Tōnen Zokusei" (当年属星). The ritual aims to honor these stars, pray for individual well-being, and eliminate misfortunes. Various temples across Japan hold Hoshi Matsuri and Star Memorial ceremonies.
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * {{ill|Iwahara Taishin|ja|岩原諦信|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwahara Taishin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, *Star Memorial Service and Its Science* (『星供と其科学』), Matsumoto Nisshindō Honten, 1942 (Shōwa 17). * {{ill|Yano Michio|ja|矢野道雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yano Michio|qq=|1=Michio Yano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11584444}}, *Esoteric Astrology - Sukuyōdō and Indian Astrology* (『密教占星術 - 宿曜道とインド占星術』), Tokyo Bijutsu, 1986 (Shōwa 61). * {{ill|Hayashi Atsushi|ja|林温|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayashi Atsushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 林ゆたか|1=Yutaka Hayashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11532783}}, *Myōken Bosatsu and the Star Mandala* (『妙見菩薩と星曼荼羅』), Japanese Art No. 377, supervised by [[:en:Agency for Cultural Affairs|Agency for Cultural Affairs]], [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]], [[:en:Kyoto National Museum|Kyoto National Museum]], and [[:en:Nara National Museum|Nara National Museum]], Shibundō, 1997. * Takeda Kazuaki, *A Study of the Star Mandala* (『星曼荼羅の研究』), Hōzōkan, 1995. == See Also == * {{ill|Sukuyōdō|ja|宿曜道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukuyōdō|qq=}} * {{ill|Manjushri Bodhisattva and the Auspicious Sutra of Good and Bad Days|ja|文殊師利菩薩及諸仙所説吉凶時日善悪宿曜経|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjushri Bodhisattva and the Auspicious Sutra of Good and Bad Days|qq=|1=Sūtra on the Astrology of Auspicious and Inauspicious Days and Good and Evil Spoken by Mañjuśrī Bodhisattva and Celestial Beings|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500028}} * [[:en:Big Dipper|Big Dipper]] * [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] * [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]] * [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]] * {{ill|Agon Star Festival|ja|阿含の星まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agon Star Festival|qq=|1=Agon Star Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657346}} * {{ill|Goshichinichi Mishuhō|ja|後七日御修法|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goshichinichi Mishuhō|qq=|1=Goshichinichi Mishiho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124680869}}
{{Esoteric Buddhism}} {{Buddhism}} {{Buddhism-stub}}

# An alternative name for [[:en:Qixi Festival|Tanabata]]. For details, see the corresponding article. # A colloquial term for "Star Memorial Service" (星供養). This article focuses on this topic.
== Overview ==
[[File:Star Mandala (Horyuji).jpg|thumb|Star Mandala ([[:en:Hōryū-ji|Hōryū-ji]] Collection)]]
'''Hoshi Matsuri''' (星まつり or 星祭り) is a [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhist]] ritual held on occasions such as the Lunar New Year, [[:en:Lichun|Risshun]], and [[:en:Winter solstice|Winter Solstice]].<ref>In Taoism, the ritual was originally conducted on the Winter Solstice and had no connection to Risshun or the Lunar New Year. It was only after being incorporated into Buddhism that it began to be observed on these additional dates.</ref> The purpose of this ritual is to ward off various disasters affecting both the state and individuals. It is also known as "'''Star Memorial Service'''” (星供養, Hoshikuyō), "'''Star Offering'''” (星供, Hoshiku), or the "'''Big Dipper Ritual'''” (北斗法, Hokutohō).
In [[:en:Mikkyō|Esoteric Buddhism]], which belongs to the later teachings of the {{ill|Mahayana|ja|大乗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mahayana|qq=|1=Mahayana|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18989147}} tradition, deities such as {{ill|Ichiji Kinrin Butchō|ja|一字金輪仏頂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiji Kinrin Butchō|qq=|1=Ekākṣaroṣṇīṣacakra|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352518}} (also known as {{ill|Dainichi Kinrin|ja|大日金輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainichi Kinrin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Shaka Kinrin|ja|釈迦金輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaka Kinrin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) are enshrined in mandalas like the {{ill|Star Mandala|ja|星曼荼羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star Mandala|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Other examples include the {{ill|Seven-Star Nyoirin Mandala|ja|七星如意輪曼荼羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven-Star Nyoirin Mandala|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} with [[:en:Cintāmaṇicakra|Nyoirin Kannon]] as the principal deity and the {{ill|Myōken Mandala|ja|妙見曼荼羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Mandala|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} featuring [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]]. These mandalas are central to rituals and ceremonies that honor the year's guiding star (当年星, tōnenjō)<ref>"Tōnenjō" is also referred to as "Tōnen Zokusei" (当年属星).</ref> and the natal star (本命星, honmyōjō).<ref>Although the term "honmyōsei" is also used in [[:en:Flying Star Feng Shui|Nine Star Ki]], in this context it refers to stars from the [[:en:Big Dipper|Big Dipper]] and the {{ill|Nine Luminaries|ja|九曜星|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nine Luminaries|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九曜|1=Navagraha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1078632}}, rather than the nine stars of Ki astrology.</ref>
Originally, this ritual was part of the [[:en:Taoism|Taoist]] Winter Solstice festival and had no relation to {{ill|Indian Buddhism|ja|インドの仏教|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Indian Buddhism|qq=|1=Buddhism in India|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2348029}}. However, it was later incorporated into Esoteric Buddhism, where it was adapted and reinterpreted within a Buddhist framework.
In Esoteric astrology, as represented by the Star Mandala, both esoteric rituals and celestial astrology form the core principles. One of the nine [[:en:Navagraha|Navagraha]] stars or the seven stars of the Big Dipper is assigned as a person's natal star (本命星), believed to govern their destiny.
The star that influences a person's fortune for the year is called the "Tōnen Zokusei" (当年属星). The ritual aims to honor these stars, pray for individual well-being, and eliminate misfortunes. Various temples across Japan hold Hoshi Matsuri and Star Memorial ceremonies.
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * {{ill|Iwahara Taishin|ja|岩原諦信|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwahara Taishin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, *Star Memorial Service and Its Science* (『星供と其科学』), Matsumoto Nisshindō Honten, 1942 (Shōwa 17). * {{ill|Yano Michio|ja|矢野道雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yano Michio|qq=|1=Michio Yano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11584444}}, *Esoteric Astrology - Sukuyōdō and Indian Astrology* (『密教占星術 - 宿曜道とインド占星術』), Tokyo Bijutsu, 1986 (Shōwa 61). * {{ill|Hayashi Atsushi|ja|林温|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayashi Atsushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 林ゆたか|1=Yutaka Hayashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11532783}}, *Myōken Bosatsu and the Star Mandala* (『妙見菩薩と星曼荼羅』), Japanese Art No. 377, supervised by [[:en:Agency for Cultural Affairs|Agency for Cultural Affairs]], [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]], [[:en:Kyoto National Museum|Kyoto National Museum]], and [[:en:Nara National Museum|Nara National Museum]], Shibundō, 1997. * Takeda Kazuaki, *A Study of the Star Mandala* (『星曼荼羅の研究』), Hōzōkan, 1995. == See Also == * {{ill|Sukuyōdō|ja|宿曜道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukuyōdō|qq=}} * {{ill|Manjushri Bodhisattva and the Auspicious Sutra of Good and Bad Days|ja|文殊師利菩薩及諸仙所説吉凶時日善悪宿曜経|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjushri Bodhisattva and the Auspicious Sutra of Good and Bad Days|qq=|1=Sūtra on the Astrology of Auspicious and Inauspicious Days and Good and Evil Spoken by Mañjuśrī Bodhisattva and Celestial Beings|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500028}} * [[:en:Big Dipper|Big Dipper]] * [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] * [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]] * [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]] * {{ill|Agon Star Festival|ja|阿含の星まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agon Star Festival|qq=|1=Agon Star Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657346}} * {{ill|Goshichinichi Mishuhō|ja|後七日御修法|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goshichinichi Mishuhō|qq=|1=Goshichinichi Mishiho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124680869}}

== Overview ==
[[File:Star Mandala (Horyuji).jpg|thumb|Star Mandala ([[:en:Hōryū-ji|Hōryū-ji]] Collection)]]
'''Hoshi Matsuri''' (星まつり or 星祭り) is a [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhist]] ritual held on occasions such as the Lunar New Year, [[:en:Lichun|Risshun]], and [[:en:Winter solstice|Winter Solstice]].<ref>In Taoism, the ritual was originally conducted on the Winter Solstice and had no connection to Risshun or the Lunar New Year. It was only after being incorporated into Buddhism that it began to be observed on these additional dates.</ref> The purpose of this ritual is to ward off various disasters affecting both the state and individuals. It is also known as "'''Star Memorial Service'''” (星供養, Hoshikuyō), "'''Star Offering'''” (星供, Hoshiku), or the "'''Big Dipper Ritual'''” (北斗法, Hokutohō).
In [[:en:Mikkyō|Esoteric Buddhism]], which belongs to the later teachings of the {{ill|Mahayana|ja|大乗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mahayana|qq=|1=Mahayana|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18989147}} tradition, deities such as {{ill|Ichiji Kinrin Butchō|ja|一字金輪仏頂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiji Kinrin Butchō|qq=|1=Ekākṣaroṣṇīṣacakra|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352518}} (also known as {{ill|Dainichi Kinrin|ja|大日金輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainichi Kinrin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Shaka Kinrin|ja|釈迦金輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaka Kinrin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) are enshrined in mandalas like the {{ill|Star Mandala|ja|星曼荼羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star Mandala|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Other examples include the {{ill|Seven-Star Nyoirin Mandala|ja|七星如意輪曼荼羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven-Star Nyoirin Mandala|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} with [[:en:Cintāmaṇicakra|Nyoirin Kannon]] as the principal deity and the {{ill|Myōken Mandala|ja|妙見曼荼羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Mandala|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} featuring [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]]. These mandalas are central to rituals and ceremonies that honor the year's guiding star (当年星, tōnenjō)<ref>"Tōnenjō" is also referred to as "Tōnen Zokusei" (当年属星).</ref> and the natal star (本命星, honmyōjō).<ref>Although the term "honmyōsei" is also used in [[:en:Flying Star Feng Shui|Nine Star Ki]], in this context it refers to stars from the [[:en:Big Dipper|Big Dipper]] and the {{ill|Nine Luminaries|ja|九曜星|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nine Luminaries|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九曜|1=Navagraha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1078632}}, rather than the nine stars of Ki astrology.</ref>
Originally, this ritual was part of the [[:en:Taoism|Taoist]] Winter Solstice festival and had no relation to {{ill|Indian Buddhism|ja|インドの仏教|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Indian Buddhism|qq=|1=Buddhism in India|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2348029}}. However, it was later incorporated into Esoteric Buddhism, where it was adapted and reinterpreted within a Buddhist framework.
In Esoteric astrology, as represented by the Star Mandala, both esoteric rituals and celestial astrology form the core principles. One of the nine [[:en:Navagraha|Navagraha]] stars or the seven stars of the Big Dipper is assigned as a person's natal star (本命星), believed to govern their destiny.
The star that influences a person's fortune for the year is called the "Tōnen Zokusei" (当年属星). The ritual aims to honor these stars, pray for individual well-being, and eliminate misfortunes. Various temples across Japan hold Hoshi Matsuri and Star Memorial ceremonies.
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * {{ill|Iwahara Taishin|ja|岩原諦信|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwahara Taishin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, *Star Memorial Service and Its Science* (『星供と其科学』), Matsumoto Nisshindō Honten, 1942 (Shōwa 17). * {{ill|Yano Michio|ja|矢野道雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yano Michio|qq=|1=Michio Yano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11584444}}, *Esoteric Astrology - Sukuyōdō and Indian Astrology* (『密教占星術 - 宿曜道とインド占星術』), Tokyo Bijutsu, 1986 (Shōwa 61). * {{ill|Hayashi Atsushi|ja|林温|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayashi Atsushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 林ゆたか|1=Yutaka Hayashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11532783}}, *Myōken Bosatsu and the Star Mandala* (『妙見菩薩と星曼荼羅』), Japanese Art No. 377, supervised by [[:en:Agency for Cultural Affairs|Agency for Cultural Affairs]], [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]], [[:en:Kyoto National Museum|Kyoto National Museum]], and [[:en:Nara National Museum|Nara National Museum]], Shibundō, 1997. * Takeda Kazuaki, *A Study of the Star Mandala* (『星曼荼羅の研究』), Hōzōkan, 1995. == See Also == * {{ill|Sukuyōdō|ja|宿曜道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukuyōdō|qq=}} * {{ill|Manjushri Bodhisattva and the Auspicious Sutra of Good and Bad Days|ja|文殊師利菩薩及諸仙所説吉凶時日善悪宿曜経|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjushri Bodhisattva and the Auspicious Sutra of Good and Bad Days|qq=|1=Sūtra on the Astrology of Auspicious and Inauspicious Days and Good and Evil Spoken by Mañjuśrī Bodhisattva and Celestial Beings|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500028}} * [[:en:Big Dipper|Big Dipper]] * [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] * [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]] * [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]] * {{ill|Agon Star Festival|ja|阿含の星まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agon Star Festival|qq=|1=Agon Star Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657346}} * {{ill|Goshichinichi Mishuhō|ja|後七日御修法|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goshichinichi Mishuhō|qq=|1=Goshichinichi Mishiho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124680869}}

[[File:Star Mandala (Horyuji).jpg|thumb|Star Mandala ([[:en:Hōryū-ji|Hōryū-ji]] Collection)]]

In [[:en:Mikkyō|Esoteric Buddhism]], which belongs to the later teachings of the {{ill|Mahayana|ja|大乗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mahayana|qq=|1=Mahayana|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18989147}} tradition, deities such as {{ill|Ichiji Kinrin Butchō|ja|一字金輪仏頂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiji Kinrin Butchō|qq=|1=Ekākṣaroṣṇīṣacakra|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352518}} (also known as {{ill|Dainichi Kinrin|ja|大日金輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainichi Kinrin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Shaka Kinrin|ja|釈迦金輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaka Kinrin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) are enshrined in mandalas like the {{ill|Star Mandala|ja|星曼荼羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star Mandala|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Other examples include the {{ill|Seven-Star Nyoirin Mandala|ja|七星如意輪曼荼羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven-Star Nyoirin Mandala|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} with [[:en:Cintāmaṇicakra|Nyoirin Kannon]] as the principal deity and the {{ill|Myōken Mandala|ja|妙見曼荼羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Mandala|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} featuring [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]]. However, it was later incorporated into Esoteric Buddhism, where it was adapted and reinterpreted within a Buddhist framework.

== Overview ==
[[File:Star Mandala (Horyuji).jpg|thumb|Star Mandala ([[:en:Hōryū-ji|Hōryū-ji]] Collection)]]
'''Hoshi Matsuri''' (星まつり or 星祭り) is a [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhist]] ritual held on occasions such as the Lunar New Year, [[:en:Lichun|Risshun]], and [[:en:Winter solstice|Winter Solstice]].<ref>In Taoism, the ritual was originally conducted on the Winter Solstice and had no connection to Risshun or the Lunar New Year. It was only after being incorporated into Buddhism that it began to be observed on these additional dates.</ref> The purpose of this ritual is to ward off various disasters affecting both the state and individuals. It is also known as "'''Star Memorial Service'''” (星供養, Hoshikuyō), "'''Star Offering'''” (星供, Hoshiku), or the "'''Big Dipper Ritual'''” (北斗法, Hokutohō).
In [[:en:Mikkyō|Esoteric Buddhism]], which belongs to the later teachings of the {{ill|Mahayana|ja|大乗|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mahayana|1=Mahayana|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18989147}} tradition, deities such as {{ill|Ichiji Kinrin Butchō|ja|一字金輪仏頂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiji Kinrin Butchō|1=Ekākṣaroṣṇīṣacakra|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352518}} (also known as {{ill|Dainichi Kinrin|ja|大日金輪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainichi Kinrin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Shaka Kinrin|ja|釈迦金輪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaka Kinrin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) are enshrined in mandalas like the {{ill|Star Mandala|ja|星曼荼羅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star Mandala|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Other examples include the {{ill|Seven-Star Nyoirin Mandala|ja|七星如意輪曼荼羅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven-Star Nyoirin Mandala|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} with [[:en:Cintāmaṇicakra|Nyoirin Kannon]] as the principal deity and the {{ill|Myōken Mandala|ja|妙見曼荼羅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Mandala|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} featuring [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]]. These mandalas are central to rituals and ceremonies that honor the year's guiding star (当年星, tōnenjō)<ref>"Tōnenjō" is also referred to as "Tōnen Zokusei" (当年属星).</ref> and the natal star (本命星, honmyōjō).<ref>Although the term "honmyōsei" is also used in [[:en:Flying Star Feng Shui|Nine Star Ki]], in this context it refers to stars from the [[:en:Big Dipper|Big Dipper]] and the {{ill|Nine Luminaries|ja|九曜星|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nine Luminaries|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九曜|1=Navagraha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1078632}}, rather than the nine stars of Ki astrology.</ref>
Originally, this ritual was part of the [[:en:Taoism|Taoist]] Winter Solstice festival and had no relation to {{ill|Indian Buddhism|ja|インドの仏教|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Indian Buddhism|1=Buddhism in India|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2348029}}. However, it was later incorporated into Esoteric Buddhism, where it was adapted and reinterpreted within a Buddhist framework.
In Esoteric astrology, as represented by the Star Mandala, both esoteric rituals and celestial astrology form the core principles. One of the nine [[:en:Navagraha|Navagraha]] stars or the seven stars of the Big Dipper is assigned as a person's natal star (本命星), believed to govern their destiny.
The star that influences a person's fortune for the year is called the "Tōnen Zokusei" (当年属星). The ritual aims to honor these stars, pray for individual well-being, and eliminate misfortunes. Various temples across Japan hold Hoshi Matsuri and Star Memorial ceremonies.

Sukuyōdō and Indian Astrology* (『密教占星術 - 宿曜道とインド占星術』), Tokyo Bijutsu, 1986 (Shōwa 61). * {{ill|Hayashi Atsushi|ja|林温|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayashi Atsushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 林ゆたか|1=Yutaka Hayashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11532783}}, *Myōken Bosatsu and the Star Mandala* (『妙見菩薩と星曼荼羅』), Japanese Art No. 377, supervised by [[:en:Agency for Cultural Affairs|Agency for Cultural Affairs]], [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]], [[:en:Kyoto National Museum|Kyoto National Museum]], and [[:en:Nara National Museum|Nara National Museum]], Shibundō, 1997. * Takeda Kazuaki, *A Study of the Star Mandala* (『星曼荼羅の研究』), Hōzōkan, 1995. == See Also == * {{ill|Sukuyōdō|ja|宿曜道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukuyōdō|qq=}} * {{ill|Manjushri Bodhisattva and the Auspicious Sutra of Good and Bad Days|ja|文殊師利菩薩及諸仙所説吉凶時日善悪宿曜経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjushri Bodhisattva and the Auspicious Sutra of Good and Bad Days|1=Sūtra on the Astrology of Auspicious and Inauspicious Days and Good and Evil Spoken by Mañjuśrī Bodhisattva and Celestial Beings|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500028}} * [[:en:Big Dipper|Big Dipper]] * [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] * [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]] * [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]] * {{ill|Agon Star Festival|ja|阿含の星まつり|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agon Star Festival|1=Agon Star Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657346}} * {{ill|Goshichinichi Mishuhō|ja|後七日御修法|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goshichinichi Mishuhō|1=Goshichinichi Mishiho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124680869}}

In [[:en:Mikkyō|Esoteric Buddhism]], which belongs to the later teachings of the {{ill|Mahayana|ja|大乗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mahayana|1=Mahayana|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18989147}} tradition, deities such as {{ill|Ichiji Kinrin Butchō|ja|一字金輪仏頂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiji Kinrin Butchō|1=Ekākṣaroṣṇīṣacakra|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352518}} (also known as {{ill|Dainichi Kinrin|ja|大日金輪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainichi Kinrin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Shaka Kinrin|ja|釈迦金輪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaka Kinrin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) are enshrined in mandalas like the {{ill|Star Mandala|ja|星曼荼羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star Mandala|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Other examples include the {{ill|Seven-Star Nyoirin Mandala|ja|七星如意輪曼荼羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven-Star Nyoirin Mandala|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} with [[:en:Cintāmaṇicakra|Nyoirin Kannon]] as the principal deity and the {{ill|Myōken Mandala|ja|妙見曼荼羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Mandala|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} featuring [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]]. These mandalas are central to rituals and ceremonies that honor the year's guiding star (当年星, tōnenjō)<ref>"Tōnenjō" is also referred to as "Tōnen Zokusei" (当年属星).</ref> and the natal star (本命星, honmyōjō).<ref>Although the term "honmyōsei" is also used in [[:en:Flying Star Feng Shui|Nine Star Ki]], in this context it refers to stars from the [[:en:Big Dipper|Big Dipper]] and the {{ill|Nine Luminaries|ja|九曜星|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nine Luminaries|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九曜|1=Navagraha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1078632}}, rather than the nine stars of Ki astrology.</ref>
Originally, this ritual was part of the [[:en:Taoism|Taoist]] Winter Solstice festival and had no relation to {{ill|Indian Buddhism|ja|インドの仏教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Indian Buddhism|1=Buddhism in India|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2348029}}. However, it was later incorporated into Esoteric Buddhism, where it was adapted and reinterpreted within a Buddhist framework.
In Esoteric astrology, as represented by the Star Mandala, both esoteric rituals and celestial astrology form the core principles. One of the nine [[:en:Navagraha|Navagraha]] stars or the seven stars of the Big Dipper is assigned as a person's natal star (本命星), believed to govern their destiny.
The star that influences a person's fortune for the year is called the "Tōnen Zokusei" (当年属星). The ritual aims to honor these stars, pray for individual well-being, and eliminate misfortunes. Various temples across Japan hold Hoshi Matsuri and Star Memorial ceremonies.

Other examples include the {{ill|Seven-Star Nyoirin Mandala|ja|七星如意輪曼荼羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven-Star Nyoirin Mandala|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} with [[:en:Cintāmaṇicakra|Nyoirin Kannon]] as the principal deity and the {{ill|Myōken Mandala|ja|妙見曼荼羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Mandala|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} featuring [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]]. These mandalas are central to rituals and ceremonies that honor the year's guiding star (当年星, tōnenjō)<ref>"Tōnenjō" is also referred to as "Tōnen Zokusei" (当年属星).</ref> and the natal star (本命星, honmyōjō).<ref>Although the term "honmyōsei" is also used in [[:en:Flying Star Feng Shui|Nine Star Ki]], in this context it refers to stars from the [[:en:Big Dipper|Big Dipper]] and the {{ill|Nine Luminaries|ja|九曜星|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nine Luminaries|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九曜|1=Navagraha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1078632}}, rather than the nine stars of Ki astrology.</ref>
Originally, this ritual was part of the [[:en:Taoism|Taoist]] Winter Solstice festival and had no relation to {{ill|Indian Buddhism|ja|インドの仏教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Indian Buddhism|1=Buddhism in India|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2348029}}. However, it was later incorporated into Esoteric Buddhism, where it was adapted and reinterpreted within a Buddhist framework.
In Esoteric astrology, as represented by the Star Mandala, both esoteric rituals and celestial astrology form the core principles. One of the nine [[:en:Navagraha|Navagraha]] stars or the seven stars of the Big Dipper is assigned as a person's natal star (本命星), believed to govern their destiny.
The star that influences a person's fortune for the year is called the "Tōnen Zokusei" (当年属星). The ritual aims to honor these stars, pray for individual well-being, and eliminate misfortunes. Various temples across Japan hold Hoshi Matsuri and Star Memorial ceremonies.

It was only after being incorporated into Buddhism that it began to be observed on these additional dates.</ref> The purpose of this ritual is to ward off various disasters affecting both the state and individuals. In [[:en:Mikkyō|Esoteric Buddhism]], which belongs to the later teachings of the {{ill|Mahayana|ja|大乗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mahayana|1=Mahayana|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18989147}} tradition, deities such as {{ill|Ichiji Kinrin Butchō|ja|一字金輪仏頂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiji Kinrin Butchō|1=Ekākṣaroṣṇīṣacakra|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352518}} (also known as {{ill|Dainichi Kinrin|ja|大日金輪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainichi Kinrin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Shaka Kinrin|ja|釈迦金輪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaka Kinrin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) are enshrined in mandalas like the {{ill|Star Mandala|ja|星曼荼羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star Mandala|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Other examples include the {{ill|Seven-Star Nyoirin Mandala|ja|七星如意輪曼荼羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven-Star Nyoirin Mandala|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} with [[:en:Cintāmaṇicakra|Nyoirin Kannon]] as the principal deity and the {{ill|Myōken Mandala|ja|妙見曼荼羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Mandala|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} featuring [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]]. These mandalas are central to rituals and ceremonies that honor the year's guiding star (当年星, tōnenjō)<ref>"Tōnenjō" is also referred to as "Tōnen Zokusei" (当年属星).</ref> and the natal star (本命星, honmyōjō).<ref>Although the term "honmyōsei" is also used in [[:en:Flying Star Feng Shui|Nine Star Ki]], in this context it refers to stars from the [[:en:Big Dipper|Big Dipper]] and the {{ill|Nine Luminaries|ja|九曜星|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nine Luminaries|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九曜|1=Navagraha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1078632}}, rather than the nine stars of Ki astrology.</ref>
Originally, this ritual was part of the [[:en:Taoism|Taoist]] Winter Solstice festival and had no relation to {{ill|Indian Buddhism|ja|インドの仏教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Indian Buddhism|1=Buddhism in India|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2348029}}. However, it was later incorporated into Esoteric Buddhism, where it was adapted and reinterpreted within a Buddhist framework.
In Esoteric astrology, as represented by the Star Mandala, both esoteric rituals and celestial astrology form the core principles. One of the nine [[:en:Navagraha|Navagraha]] stars or the seven stars of the Big Dipper is assigned as a person's natal star (本命星), believed to govern their destiny.
The star that influences a person's fortune for the year is called the "Tōnen Zokusei" (当年属星). The ritual aims to honor these stars, pray for individual well-being, and eliminate misfortunes. Various temples across Japan hold Hoshi Matsuri and Star Memorial ceremonies.

== Overview ==
[[File:Star Mandala (Horyuji).jpg|thumb|Star Mandala ([[:en:Hōryū-ji|Hōryū-ji]] Collection)]]
'''Hoshi Matsuri''' (星まつり or 星祭り) is a [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhist]] ritual held on occasions such as the Lunar New Year, [[:en:Lichun|Risshun]], and [[:en:Winter solstice|Winter Solstice]].<ref>In Taoism, the ritual was originally conducted on the Winter Solstice and had no connection to Risshun or the Lunar New Year. It was only after being incorporated into Buddhism that it began to be observed on these additional dates.</ref> The purpose of this ritual is to ward off various disasters affecting both the state and individuals. It is also known as "'''Star Memorial Service'''” (星供養, Hoshikuyō), "'''Star Offering'''” (星供, Hoshiku), or the "'''Big Dipper Ritual'''” (北斗法, Hokutohō).

Other examples include the {{ill|Seven-Star Nyoirin Mandala|ja|七星如意輪曼荼羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven-Star Nyoirin Mandala|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} with [[:en:Cintāmaṇicakra|Nyoirin Kannon]] as the principal deity and the {{ill|Myōken Mandala|ja|妙見曼荼羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Mandala|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} featuring [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]]. These mandalas are central to rituals and ceremonies that honor the year's guiding star (当年星, tōnenjō)<ref>"Tōnenjō" is also referred to as "Tōnen Zokusei" (当年属星).</ref> and the natal star (本命星, honmyōjō).<ref>Although the term "honmyōsei" is also used in [[:en:Flying Star Feng Shui|Nine Star Ki]], in this context it refers to stars from the [[:en:Big Dipper|Big Dipper]] and the {{ill|Nine Luminaries|ja|九曜星|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nine Luminaries|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九曜|1=Navagraha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1078632}}, rather than the nine stars of Ki astrology.</ref>
Originally, this ritual was part of the [[:en:Taoism|Taoist]] Winter Solstice festival and had no relation to {{ill|Indian Buddhism|ja|インドの仏教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Indian Buddhism|1=Buddhism in India|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2348029}}. However, it was later incorporated into Esoteric Buddhism, where it was adapted and reinterpreted within a Buddhist framework.
In Esoteric astrology, as represented by the Star Mandala, both esoteric rituals and celestial astrology form the core principles. One of the nine [[:en:Navagraha|Navagraha]] stars or the seven stars of the Big Dipper is assigned as a person's natal star (本命星), believed to govern their destiny.
The star that influences a person's fortune for the year is called the "Tōnen Zokusei" (当年属星). The ritual aims to honor these stars, pray for individual well-being, and eliminate misfortunes. Various temples across Japan hold Hoshi Matsuri and Star Memorial ceremonies.

'''Hoshi Matsuri''' (星まつり or 星祭り) is a [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhist]] ritual held on occasions such as the Lunar New Year, [[:en:Lichun|Risshun]], and [[:en:Winter solstice|Winter Solstice]].<ref>In Taoism, the ritual was originally conducted on the Winter Solstice and had no connection to Risshun or the Lunar New Year. It was only after being incorporated into Buddhism that it began to be observed on these additional dates.</ref> The purpose of this ritual is to ward off various disasters affecting both the state and individuals. It is also known as "'''Star Memorial Service'''” (星供養, Hoshikuyō), "'''Star Offering'''” (星供, Hoshiku), or the "'''Big Dipper Ritual'''” (北斗法, Hokutohō).
In [[:en:Mikkyō|Esoteric Buddhism]], which belongs to the later teachings of the {{ill|Mahayana|ja|大乗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mahayana|1=Mahayana|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18989147}} tradition, deities such as {{ill|Ichiji Kinrin Butchō|ja|一字金輪仏頂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiji Kinrin Butchō|1=Ekākṣaroṣṇīṣacakra|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352518}} (also known as {{ill|Dainichi Kinrin|ja|大日金輪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainichi Kinrin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Shaka Kinrin|ja|釈迦金輪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaka Kinrin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) are enshrined in mandalas like the {{ill|Star Mandala|ja|星曼荼羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star Mandala|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Other examples include the {{ill|Seven-Star Nyoirin Mandala|ja|七星如意輪曼荼羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven-Star Nyoirin Mandala|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} with [[:en:Cintāmaṇicakra|Nyoirin Kannon]] as the principal deity and the {{ill|Myōken Mandala|ja|妙見曼荼羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Mandala|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} featuring [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]]. These mandalas are central to rituals and ceremonies that honor the year's guiding star (当年星, tōnenjō)<ref>"Tōnenjō" is also referred to as "Tōnen Zokusei" (当年属星).</ref> and the natal star (本命星, honmyōjō).<ref>Although the term "honmyōsei" is also used in [[:en:Flying Star Feng Shui|Nine Star Ki]], in this context it refers to stars from the [[:en:Big Dipper|Big Dipper]] and the {{ill|Nine Luminaries|ja|九曜星|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nine Luminaries|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九曜|1=Navagraha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1078632}}, rather than the nine stars of Ki astrology.</ref>
Originally, this ritual was part of the [[:en:Taoism|Taoist]] Winter Solstice festival and had no relation to {{ill|Indian Buddhism|ja|インドの仏教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Indian Buddhism|1=Buddhism in India|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2348029}}. However, it was later incorporated into Esoteric Buddhism, where it was adapted and reinterpreted within a Buddhist framework.
In Esoteric astrology, as represented by the Star Mandala, both esoteric rituals and celestial astrology form the core principles. One of the nine [[:en:Navagraha|Navagraha]] stars or the seven stars of the Big Dipper is assigned as a person's natal star (本命星), believed to govern their destiny.
The star that influences a person's fortune for the year is called the "Tōnen Zokusei" (当年属星). The ritual aims to honor these stars, pray for individual well-being, and eliminate misfortunes. Various temples across Japan hold Hoshi Matsuri and Star Memorial ceremonies.
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * {{ill|Iwahara Taishin|ja|岩原諦信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwahara Taishin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, *Star Memorial Service and Its Science* (『星供と其科学』), Matsumoto Nisshindō Honten, 1942 (Shōwa 17). * {{ill|Yano Michio|ja|矢野道雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yano Michio|1=Michio Yano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11584444}}, *Esoteric Astrology - Sukuyōdō and Indian Astrology* (『密教占星術 - 宿曜道とインド占星術』), Tokyo Bijutsu, 1986 (Shōwa 61). * {{ill|Hayashi Atsushi|ja|林温|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayashi Atsushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 林ゆたか|1=Yutaka Hayashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11532783}}, *Myōken Bosatsu and the Star Mandala* (『妙見菩薩と星曼荼羅』), Japanese Art No. 377, supervised by [[:en:Agency for Cultural Affairs|Agency for Cultural Affairs]], [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]], [[:en:Kyoto National Museum|Kyoto National Museum]], and [[:en:Nara National Museum|Nara National Museum]], Shibundō, 1997. * Takeda Kazuaki, *A Study of the Star Mandala* (『星曼荼羅の研究』), Hōzōkan, 1995. == See Also == * {{ill|Sukuyōdō|ja|宿曜道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukuyōdō|qq=}} * {{ill|Manjushri Bodhisattva and the Auspicious Sutra of Good and Bad Days|ja|文殊師利菩薩及諸仙所説吉凶時日善悪宿曜経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjushri Bodhisattva and the Auspicious Sutra of Good and Bad Days|1=Sūtra on the Astrology of Auspicious and Inauspicious Days and Good and Evil Spoken by Mañjuśrī Bodhisattva and Celestial Beings|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500028}} * [[:en:Big Dipper|Big Dipper]] * [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] * [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]] * [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]] * {{ill|Agon Star Festival|ja|阿含の星まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agon Star Festival|1=Agon Star Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657346}} * {{ill|Goshichinichi Mishuhō|ja|後七日御修法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goshichinichi Mishuhō|1=Goshichinichi Mishiho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124680869}}

It was only after being incorporated into Buddhism that it began to be observed on these additional dates.</ref> The purpose of this ritual is to ward off various disasters affecting both the state and individuals. It is also known as "'''Star Memorial Service'''” (星供養, Hoshikuyō), "'''Star Offering'''” (星供, Hoshiku), or the "'''Big Dipper Ritual'''” (北斗法, Hokutohō).
In [[:en:Mikkyō|Esoteric Buddhism]], which belongs to the later teachings of the {{ill|Mahayana|ja|大乗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mahayana|1=Mahayana|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18989147}} tradition, deities such as {{ill|Ichiji Kinrin Butchō|ja|一字金輪仏頂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiji Kinrin Butchō|1=Ekākṣaroṣṇīṣacakra|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352518}} (also known as {{ill|Dainichi Kinrin|ja|大日金輪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainichi Kinrin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Shaka Kinrin|ja|釈迦金輪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaka Kinrin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) are enshrined in mandalas like the {{ill|Star Mandala|ja|星曼荼羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star Mandala|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Other examples include the {{ill|Seven-Star Nyoirin Mandala|ja|七星如意輪曼荼羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven-Star Nyoirin Mandala|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} with [[:en:Cintāmaṇicakra|Nyoirin Kannon]] as the principal deity and the {{ill|Myōken Mandala|ja|妙見曼荼羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Mandala|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} featuring [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]]. These mandalas are central to rituals and ceremonies that honor the year's guiding star (当年星, tōnenjō)<ref>"Tōnenjō" is also referred to as "Tōnen Zokusei" (当年属星).</ref> and the natal star (本命星, honmyōjō).<ref>Although the term "honmyōsei" is also used in [[:en:Flying Star Feng Shui|Nine Star Ki]], in this context it refers to stars from the [[:en:Big Dipper|Big Dipper]] and the {{ill|Nine Luminaries|ja|九曜星|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nine Luminaries|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九曜|1=Navagraha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1078632}}, rather than the nine stars of Ki astrology.</ref>
Originally, this ritual was part of the [[:en:Taoism|Taoist]] Winter Solstice festival and had no relation to {{ill|Indian Buddhism|ja|インドの仏教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Indian Buddhism|1=Buddhism in India|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2348029}}. However, it was later incorporated into Esoteric Buddhism, where it was adapted and reinterpreted within a Buddhist framework.
In Esoteric astrology, as represented by the Star Mandala, both esoteric rituals and celestial astrology form the core principles. One of the nine [[:en:Navagraha|Navagraha]] stars or the seven stars of the Big Dipper is assigned as a person's natal star (本命星), believed to govern their destiny.
The star that influences a person's fortune for the year is called the "Tōnen Zokusei" (当年属星). The ritual aims to honor these stars, pray for individual well-being, and eliminate misfortunes. Various temples across Japan hold Hoshi Matsuri and Star Memorial ceremonies.
