Other examples include the {{ill|Seven-Star Nyoirin Mandala|ja|七星如意輪曼荼羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven-Star Nyoirin Mandala|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} with [[:en:Cintāmaṇicakra|Nyoirin Kannon]] as the principal deity and the {{ill|Myōken Mandala|ja|妙見曼荼羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Mandala|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} featuring [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]]. These mandalas are central to rituals and ceremonies that honor the year's guiding star (当年星, tōnenjō)<ref>"Tōnenjō" is also referred to as "Tōnen Zokusei" (当年属星).</ref> and the natal star (本命星, honmyōjō).<ref>Although the term "honmyōsei" is also used in [[:en:Flying Star Feng Shui|Nine Star Ki]], in this context it refers to stars from the [[:en:Big Dipper|Big Dipper]] and the {{ill|Nine Luminaries|ja|九曜星|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nine Luminaries|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九曜|1=Navagraha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1078632}}, rather than the nine stars of Ki astrology.</ref>
Originally, this ritual was part of the [[:en:Taoism|Taoist]] Winter Solstice festival and had no relation to {{ill|Indian Buddhism|ja|インドの仏教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Indian Buddhism|1=Buddhism in India|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2348029}}. However, it was later incorporated into Esoteric Buddhism, where it was adapted and reinterpreted within a Buddhist framework.
In Esoteric astrology, as represented by the Star Mandala, both esoteric rituals and celestial astrology form the core principles. One of the nine [[:en:Navagraha|Navagraha]] stars or the seven stars of the Big Dipper is assigned as a person's natal star (本命星), believed to govern their destiny.
The star that influences a person's fortune for the year is called the "Tōnen Zokusei" (当年属星). The ritual aims to honor these stars, pray for individual well-being, and eliminate misfortunes. Various temples across Japan hold Hoshi Matsuri and Star Memorial ceremonies.

In [[:en:Mikkyō|Esoteric Buddhism]], which belongs to the later teachings of the {{ill|Mahayana|ja|大乗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mahayana|1=Mahayana|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18989147}} tradition, deities such as {{ill|Ichiji Kinrin Butchō|ja|一字金輪仏頂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiji Kinrin Butchō|1=Ekākṣaroṣṇīṣacakra|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352518}} (also known as {{ill|Dainichi Kinrin|ja|大日金輪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainichi Kinrin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Shaka Kinrin|ja|釈迦金輪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaka Kinrin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) are enshrined in mandalas like the {{ill|Star Mandala|ja|星曼荼羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star Mandala|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Other examples include the {{ill|Seven-Star Nyoirin Mandala|ja|七星如意輪曼荼羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven-Star Nyoirin Mandala|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} with [[:en:Cintāmaṇicakra|Nyoirin Kannon]] as the principal deity and the {{ill|Myōken Mandala|ja|妙見曼荼羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Mandala|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} featuring [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]]. These mandalas are central to rituals and ceremonies that honor the year's guiding star (当年星, tōnenjō)<ref>"Tōnenjō" is also referred to as "Tōnen Zokusei" (当年属星).</ref> and the natal star (本命星, honmyōjō).<ref>Although the term "honmyōsei" is also used in [[:en:Flying Star Feng Shui|Nine Star Ki]], in this context it refers to stars from the [[:en:Big Dipper|Big Dipper]] and the {{ill|Nine Luminaries|ja|九曜星|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nine Luminaries|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九曜|1=Navagraha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1078632}}, rather than the nine stars of Ki astrology.</ref>
Originally, this ritual was part of the [[:en:Taoism|Taoist]] Winter Solstice festival and had no relation to {{ill|Indian Buddhism|ja|インドの仏教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Indian Buddhism|1=Buddhism in India|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2348029}}. However, it was later incorporated into Esoteric Buddhism, where it was adapted and reinterpreted within a Buddhist framework.
In Esoteric astrology, as represented by the Star Mandala, both esoteric rituals and celestial astrology form the core principles. One of the nine [[:en:Navagraha|Navagraha]] stars or the seven stars of the Big Dipper is assigned as a person's natal star (本命星), believed to govern their destiny.
The star that influences a person's fortune for the year is called the "Tōnen Zokusei" (当年属星). The ritual aims to honor these stars, pray for individual well-being, and eliminate misfortunes. Various temples across Japan hold Hoshi Matsuri and Star Memorial ceremonies.

It was only after being incorporated into Buddhism that it began to be observed on these additional dates.</ref> The purpose of this ritual is to ward off various disasters affecting both the state and individuals. It is also known as "'''Star Memorial Service'''” (星供養, Hoshikuyō), "'''Star Offering'''” (星供, Hoshiku), or the "'''Big Dipper Ritual'''” (北斗法, Hokutohō).
In [[:en:Mikkyō|Esoteric Buddhism]], which belongs to the later teachings of the {{ill|Mahayana|ja|大乗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mahayana|1=Mahayana|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18989147}} tradition, deities such as {{ill|Ichiji Kinrin Butchō|ja|一字金輪仏頂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiji Kinrin Butchō|1=Ekākṣaroṣṇīṣacakra|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352518}} (also known as {{ill|Dainichi Kinrin|ja|大日金輪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainichi Kinrin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Shaka Kinrin|ja|釈迦金輪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaka Kinrin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) are enshrined in mandalas like the {{ill|Star Mandala|ja|星曼荼羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star Mandala|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Other examples include the {{ill|Seven-Star Nyoirin Mandala|ja|七星如意輪曼荼羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven-Star Nyoirin Mandala|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} with [[:en:Cintāmaṇicakra|Nyoirin Kannon]] as the principal deity and the {{ill|Myōken Mandala|ja|妙見曼荼羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Mandala|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} featuring [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]]. These mandalas are central to rituals and ceremonies that honor the year's guiding star (当年星, tōnenjō)<ref>"Tōnenjō" is also referred to as "Tōnen Zokusei" (当年属星).</ref> and the natal star (本命星, honmyōjō).<ref>Although the term "honmyōsei" is also used in [[:en:Flying Star Feng Shui|Nine Star Ki]], in this context it refers to stars from the [[:en:Big Dipper|Big Dipper]] and the {{ill|Nine Luminaries|ja|九曜星|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nine Luminaries|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九曜|1=Navagraha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1078632}}, rather than the nine stars of Ki astrology.</ref>
Originally, this ritual was part of the [[:en:Taoism|Taoist]] Winter Solstice festival and had no relation to {{ill|Indian Buddhism|ja|インドの仏教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Indian Buddhism|1=Buddhism in India|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2348029}}. However, it was later incorporated into Esoteric Buddhism, where it was adapted and reinterpreted within a Buddhist framework.
In Esoteric astrology, as represented by the Star Mandala, both esoteric rituals and celestial astrology form the core principles. One of the nine [[:en:Navagraha|Navagraha]] stars or the seven stars of the Big Dipper is assigned as a person's natal star (本命星), believed to govern their destiny.
The star that influences a person's fortune for the year is called the "Tōnen Zokusei" (当年属星). The ritual aims to honor these stars, pray for individual well-being, and eliminate misfortunes. Various temples across Japan hold Hoshi Matsuri and Star Memorial ceremonies.

[[File:Star Mandala (Horyuji).jpg|thumb|Star Mandala ([[:en:Hōryū-ji|Hōryū-ji]] Collection)]]
'''Hoshi Matsuri''' (星まつり or 星祭り) is a [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhist]] ritual held on occasions such as the Lunar New Year, [[:en:Lichun|Risshun]], and [[:en:Winter solstice|Winter Solstice]].<ref>In Taoism, the ritual was originally conducted on the Winter Solstice and had no connection to Risshun or the Lunar New Year. It was only after being incorporated into Buddhism that it began to be observed on these additional dates.</ref> The purpose of this ritual is to ward off various disasters affecting both the state and individuals. It is also known as "'''Star Memorial Service'''” (星供養, Hoshikuyō), "'''Star Offering'''” (星供, Hoshiku), or the "'''Big Dipper Ritual'''” (北斗法, Hokutohō).
In [[:en:Mikkyō|Esoteric Buddhism]], which belongs to the later teachings of the {{ill|Mahayana|ja|大乗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mahayana|1=Mahayana|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18989147}} tradition, deities such as {{ill|Ichiji Kinrin Butchō|ja|一字金輪仏頂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiji Kinrin Butchō|1=Ekākṣaroṣṇīṣacakra|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352518}} (also known as {{ill|Dainichi Kinrin|ja|大日金輪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainichi Kinrin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Shaka Kinrin|ja|釈迦金輪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaka Kinrin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) are enshrined in mandalas like the {{ill|Star Mandala|ja|星曼荼羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star Mandala|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Other examples include the {{ill|Seven-Star Nyoirin Mandala|ja|七星如意輪曼荼羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven-Star Nyoirin Mandala|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} with [[:en:Cintāmaṇicakra|Nyoirin Kannon]] as the principal deity and the {{ill|Myōken Mandala|ja|妙見曼荼羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Mandala|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} featuring [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]]. These mandalas are central to rituals and ceremonies that honor the year's guiding star (当年星, tōnenjō)<ref>"Tōnenjō" is also referred to as "Tōnen Zokusei" (当年属星).</ref> and the natal star (本命星, honmyōjō).<ref>Although the term "honmyōsei" is also used in [[:en:Flying Star Feng Shui|Nine Star Ki]], in this context it refers to stars from the [[:en:Big Dipper|Big Dipper]] and the {{ill|Nine Luminaries|ja|九曜星|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nine Luminaries|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九曜|1=Navagraha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1078632}}, rather than the nine stars of Ki astrology.</ref>
Originally, this ritual was part of the [[:en:Taoism|Taoist]] Winter Solstice festival and had no relation to {{ill|Indian Buddhism|ja|インドの仏教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Indian Buddhism|1=Buddhism in India|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2348029}}. However, it was later incorporated into Esoteric Buddhism, where it was adapted and reinterpreted within a Buddhist framework.
In Esoteric astrology, as represented by the Star Mandala, both esoteric rituals and celestial astrology form the core principles. One of the nine [[:en:Navagraha|Navagraha]] stars or the seven stars of the Big Dipper is assigned as a person's natal star (本命星), believed to govern their destiny.
The star that influences a person's fortune for the year is called the "Tōnen Zokusei" (当年属星). The ritual aims to honor these stars, pray for individual well-being, and eliminate misfortunes. Various temples across Japan hold Hoshi Matsuri and Star Memorial ceremonies.

'''Hoshi Matsuri''' (星祭)

# An alternative name for {{ill|Qixi Festival|en|Qixi Festival|WD=Q13423122|lt=Tanabata}}. For details, see the corresponding article.
# A colloquial term for "Star Memorial Service" (星供養). This article focuses on this topic.

== Overview ==

[[File:Star Mandala (Horyuji).jpg|thumb|Star Mandala ({{ill|Hōryū-ji|en|Hōryū-ji|WD=Q261932|lt=Hōryū-ji}} Collection)]]

'''Hoshi Matsuri''' (星まつり or 星祭り) is a {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|WD=Q748|lt=Buddhist}} ritual held on occasions such as the Lunar New Year, {{ill|Lichun|en|Lichun|WD=Q1364685|lt=Risshun}}, and {{ill|Winter solstice|en|Winter solstice|WD=Q207932|lt=Winter Solstice}}.<ref>In Taoism, the ritual was originally conducted on the Winter Solstice and had no connection to Risshun or the Lunar New Year. It was only after being incorporated into Buddhism that it began to be observed on these additional dates.</ref> The purpose of this ritual is to ward off various disasters affecting both the state and individuals. It is also known as "'''Star Memorial Service'''” (星供養, Hoshikuyō), "'''Star Offering'''” (星供, Hoshiku), or the "'''Big Dipper Ritual'''” (北斗法, Hokutohō).

In {{ill|Mikkyō|en|Mikkyō|WD=Q336102|lt=Esoteric Buddhism}}, which belongs to the later teachings of the {{ill|Mahayana|ja|大乗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mahayana|1=Mahayana|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18989147}} tradition, deities such as {{ill|Ichiji Kinrin Butchō|ja|一字金輪仏頂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiji Kinrin Butchō|1=Ekākṣaroṣṇīṣacakra|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352518}} (also known as {{ill|Dainichi Kinrin|ja|大日金輪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainichi Kinrin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Shaka Kinrin|ja|釈迦金輪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaka Kinrin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) are enshrined in mandalas like the {{ill|Star Mandala|ja|星曼荼羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star Mandala|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Other examples include the {{ill|Seven-Star Nyoirin Mandala|ja|七星如意輪曼荼羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven-Star Nyoirin Mandala|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} with {{ill|Cintāmaṇicakra|en|Cintāmaṇicakra|WD=Q11446712|lt=Nyoirin Kannon}} as the principal deity and the {{ill|Myōken Mandala|ja|妙見曼荼羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Mandala|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} featuring {{ill|Myōken|en|Myōken|WD=Q10960623|lt=Myōken Bosatsu}}. These mandalas are central to rituals and ceremonies that honor the year's guiding star (当年星, tōnenjō)<ref>"Tōnenjō" is also referred to as "Tōnen Zokusei" (当年属星).</ref> and the natal star (本命星, honmyōjō).<ref>Although the term "honmyōsei" is also used in {{ill|Flying Star Feng Shui|en|Flying Star Feng Shui|WD=Q9384721|lt=Nine Star Ki}}, in this context it refers to stars from the {{ill|Big Dipper|en|Big Dipper|WD=Q10460|lt=Big Dipper}} and the {{ill|Nine Luminaries|ja|九曜星|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nine Luminaries|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九曜|1=Navagraha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1078632}}, rather than the nine stars of Ki astrology.</ref>

Originally, this ritual was part of the {{ill|Taoism|en|Taoism|WD=Q9598|lt=Taoist}} Winter Solstice festival and had no relation to {{ill|Indian Buddhism|ja|インドの仏教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Indian Buddhism|1=Buddhism in India|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2348029}}. However, it was later incorporated into Esoteric Buddhism, where it was adapted and reinterpreted within a Buddhist framework.

In Esoteric astrology, as represented by the Star Mandala, both esoteric rituals and celestial astrology form the core principles. One of the nine {{ill|Navagraha|en|Navagraha|WD=Q1078632|lt=Navagraha}} stars or the seven stars of the Big Dipper is assigned as a person's natal star (本命星), believed to govern their destiny.

The star that influences a person's fortune for the year is called the "Tōnen Zokusei" (当年属星). The ritual aims to honor these stars, pray for individual well-being, and eliminate misfortunes. Various temples across Japan hold Hoshi Matsuri and Star Memorial ceremonies.

== Overview ==

[[File:Star Mandala (Horyuji).jpg|thumb|Star Mandala ({{ill|Hōryū-ji|en|Hōryū-ji|WD=Q261932|lt=Hōryū-ji}} Collection)]]

'''Hoshi Matsuri''' (星まつり or 星祭り) is a {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|WD=Q748|lt=Buddhist}} ritual held on occasions such as the Lunar New Year, {{ill|Lichun|en|Lichun|WD=Q1364685|lt=Risshun}}, and {{ill|Winter solstice|en|Winter solstice|WD=Q207932|lt=Winter Solstice}}.<ref>In Taoism, the ritual was originally conducted on the Winter Solstice and had no connection to Risshun or the Lunar New Year. It was only after being incorporated into Buddhism that it began to be observed on these additional dates.</ref> The purpose of this ritual is to ward off various disasters affecting both the state and individuals. It is also known as "'''Star Memorial Service'''” (星供養, Hoshikuyō), "'''Star Offering'''” (星供, Hoshiku), or the "'''Big Dipper Ritual'''” (北斗法, Hokutohō).

In {{ill|Mikkyō|en|Mikkyō|WD=Q336102|lt=Esoteric Buddhism}}, which belongs to the later teachings of the {{ill|Mahayana|ja|大乗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mahayana|1=Mahayana|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18989147}} tradition, deities such as {{ill|Ichiji Kinrin Butchō|ja|一字金輪仏頂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiji Kinrin Butchō|1=Ekākṣaroṣṇīṣacakra|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352518}} (also known as {{ill|Dainichi Kinrin|ja|大日金輪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainichi Kinrin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Shaka Kinrin|ja|釈迦金輪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaka Kinrin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) are enshrined in mandalas like the {{ill|Star Mandala|ja|星曼荼羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star Mandala|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Other examples include the {{ill|Seven-Star Nyoirin Mandala|ja|七星如意輪曼荼羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven-Star Nyoirin Mandala|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} with {{ill|Cintāmaṇicakra|en|Cintāmaṇicakra|WD=Q11446712|lt=Nyoirin Kannon}} as the principal deity and the {{ill|Myōken Mandala|ja|妙見曼荼羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Mandala|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} featuring {{ill|Myōken|en|Myōken|WD=Q10960623|lt=Myōken Bosatsu}}. These mandalas are central to rituals and ceremonies that honor the year's guiding star (当年星, tōnenjō)<ref>"Tōnenjō" is also referred to as "Tōnen Zokusei" (当年属星).</ref> and the natal star (本命星, honmyōjō).<ref>Although the term "honmyōsei" is also used in {{ill|Flying Star Feng Shui|en|Flying Star Feng Shui|WD=Q9384721|lt=Nine Star Ki}}, in this context it refers to stars from the {{ill|Big Dipper|en|Big Dipper|WD=Q10460|lt=Big Dipper}} and the {{ill|Nine Luminaries|ja|九曜星|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nine Luminaries|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九曜|1=Navagraha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1078632}}, rather than the nine stars of Ki astrology.</ref>

Originally, this ritual was part of the {{ill|Taoism|en|Taoism|WD=Q9598|lt=Taoist}} Winter Solstice festival and had no relation to {{ill|Indian Buddhism|ja|インドの仏教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Indian Buddhism|1=Buddhism in India|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2348029}}. However, it was later incorporated into Esoteric Buddhism, where it was adapted and reinterpreted within a Buddhist framework.

In Esoteric astrology, as represented by the Star Mandala, both esoteric rituals and celestial astrology form the core principles. One of the nine {{ill|Navagraha|en|Navagraha|WD=Q1078632|lt=Navagraha}} stars or the seven stars of the Big Dipper is assigned as a person's natal star (本命星), believed to govern their destiny.

The star that influences a person's fortune for the year is called the "Tōnen Zokusei" (当年属星). The ritual aims to honor these stars, pray for individual well-being, and eliminate misfortunes. Various temples across Japan hold Hoshi Matsuri and Star Memorial ceremonies.

* {{ill|Yano Michio|ja|矢野道雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yano Michio|1=Michio Yano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11584444}}, *Esoteric Astrology - Sukuyōdō and Indian Astrology* (『密教占星術 - 宿曜道とインド占星術』), Tokyo Bijutsu, 1986 (Shōwa 61).
* {{ill|Hayashi Atsushi|ja|林温|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayashi Atsushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 林ゆたか|1=Yutaka Hayashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11532783}}, *Myōken Bosatsu and the Star Mandala* (『妙見菩薩と星曼荼羅』), Japanese Art No. 377, supervised by {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en|Agency for Cultural Affairs|WD=Q1009519|lt=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}, {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum}}, {{ill|Kyoto National Museum|en|Kyoto National Museum|WD=Q147286|lt=Kyoto National Museum}}, and {{ill|Nara National Museum|en|Nara National Museum|WD=Q147312|lt=Nara National Museum}}, Shibundō, 1997.
* Takeda Kazuaki, *A Study of the Star Mandala* (『星曼荼羅の研究』), Hōzōkan, 1995.

Sukuyōdō and Indian Astrology* (『密教占星術 - 宿曜道とインド占星術』), Tokyo Bijutsu, 1986 (Shōwa 61). * {{ill|Hayashi Atsushi|ja|林温|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayashi Atsushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 林ゆたか|1=Yutaka Hayashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11532783}}, *Myōken Bosatsu and the Star Mandala* (『妙見菩薩と星曼荼羅』), Japanese Art No. 377, supervised by {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en|Agency for Cultural Affairs|WD=Q1009519|lt=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}, {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum}}, {{ill|Kyoto National Museum|en|Kyoto National Museum|WD=Q147286|lt=Kyoto National Museum}}, and {{ill|Nara National Museum|en|Nara National Museum|WD=Q147312|lt=Nara National Museum}}, Shibundō, 1997. * Takeda Kazuaki, *A Study of the Star Mandala* (『星曼荼羅の研究』), Hōzōkan, 1995. == See Also == * {{ill|Sukuyōdō|ja|宿曜道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukuyōdō|qq=}} * {{ill|Manjushri Bodhisattva and the Auspicious Sutra of Good and Bad Days|ja|文殊師利菩薩及諸仙所説吉凶時日善悪宿曜経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjushri Bodhisattva and the Auspicious Sutra of Good and Bad Days|1=Sūtra on the Astrology of Auspicious and Inauspicious Days and Good and Evil Spoken by Mañjuśrī Bodhisattva and Celestial Beings|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500028}} * {{ill|Big Dipper|en|Big Dipper|WD=Q10460|lt=Big Dipper}} * {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|WD=Q1340540|lt=Onmyōdō}} * {{ill|Myōken|en|Myōken|WD=Q10960623|lt=Myōken Bosatsu}} * {{ill|Vairocana|en|Vairocana|WD=Q239847|lt=Dainichi Nyorai}} * {{ill|Agon Star Festival|ja|阿含の星まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agon Star Festival|1=Agon Star Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657346}} * {{ill|Goshichinichi Mishuhō|ja|後七日御修法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goshichinichi Mishuhō|1=Goshichinichi Mishiho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124680869}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Hoshi Matsuri]][[ja:星まつり]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Hoshi Matsuri]][[ja:星まつり]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Hoshi Matsuri]][[ja:星まつり]]
'''Hoshi Matsuri''' (星祭)
# An alternative name for {{ill|Qixi Festival|en|Qixi Festival|WD=Q13423122|lt=Tanabata}}. For details, see the corresponding article. # A colloquial term for "Star Memorial Service" (星供養). This article focuses on this topic.
== Overview ==
[[File:Star Mandala (Horyuji).jpg|thumb|Star Mandala ({{ill|Hōryū-ji|en|Hōryū-ji|WD=Q261932|lt=Hōryū-ji}} Collection)]]
'''Hoshi Matsuri''' (星まつり or 星祭り) is a {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|WD=Q748|lt=Buddhist}} ritual held on occasions such as the Lunar New Year, {{ill|Lichun|en|Lichun|WD=Q1364685|lt=Risshun}}, and {{ill|Winter solstice|en|Winter solstice|WD=Q207932|lt=Winter Solstice}}.<ref>In Taoism, the ritual was originally conducted on the Winter Solstice and had no connection to Risshun or the Lunar New Year. It was only after being incorporated into Buddhism that it began to be observed on these additional dates.</ref> The purpose of this ritual is to ward off various disasters affecting both the state and individuals. It is also known as "'''Star Memorial Service'''” (星供養, Hoshikuyō), "'''Star Offering'''” (星供, Hoshiku), or the "'''Big Dipper Ritual'''” (北斗法, Hokutohō).
In {{ill|Mikkyō|en|Mikkyō|WD=Q336102|lt=Esoteric Buddhism}}, which belongs to the later teachings of the {{ill|Mahayana|ja|大乗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mahayana|1=Mahayana|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18989147}} tradition, deities such as {{ill|Ichiji Kinrin Butchō|ja|一字金輪仏頂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiji Kinrin Butchō|1=Ekākṣaroṣṇīṣacakra|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352518}} (also known as {{ill|Dainichi Kinrin|ja|大日金輪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainichi Kinrin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Shaka Kinrin|ja|釈迦金輪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaka Kinrin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) are enshrined in mandalas like the {{ill|Star Mandala|ja|星曼荼羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star Mandala|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Other examples include the {{ill|Seven-Star Nyoirin Mandala|ja|七星如意輪曼荼羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven-Star Nyoirin Mandala|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} with {{ill|Cintāmaṇicakra|en|Cintāmaṇicakra|WD=Q11446712|lt=Nyoirin Kannon}} as the principal deity and the {{ill|Myōken Mandala|ja|妙見曼荼羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Mandala|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} featuring {{ill|Myōken|en|Myōken|WD=Q10960623|lt=Myōken Bosatsu}}. These mandalas are central to rituals and ceremonies that honor the year's guiding star (当年星, tōnenjō)<ref>"Tōnenjō" is also referred to as "Tōnen Zokusei" (当年属星).</ref> and the natal star (本命星, honmyōjō).<ref>Although the term "honmyōsei" is also used in {{ill|Flying Star Feng Shui|en|Flying Star Feng Shui|WD=Q9384721|lt=Nine Star Ki}}, in this context it refers to stars from the {{ill|Big Dipper|en|Big Dipper|WD=Q10460|lt=Big Dipper}} and the {{ill|Nine Luminaries|ja|九曜星|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nine Luminaries|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九曜|1=Navagraha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1078632}}, rather than the nine stars of Ki astrology.</ref>
Originally, this ritual was part of the {{ill|Taoism|en|Taoism|WD=Q9598|lt=Taoist}} Winter Solstice festival and had no relation to {{ill|Indian Buddhism|ja|インドの仏教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Indian Buddhism|1=Buddhism in India|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2348029}}. However, it was later incorporated into Esoteric Buddhism, where it was adapted and reinterpreted within a Buddhist framework.
In Esoteric astrology, as represented by the Star Mandala, both esoteric rituals and celestial astrology form the core principles. One of the nine {{ill|Navagraha|en|Navagraha|WD=Q1078632|lt=Navagraha}} stars or the seven stars of the Big Dipper is assigned as a person's natal star (本命星), believed to govern their destiny.
The star that influences a person's fortune for the year is called the "Tōnen Zokusei" (当年属星). The ritual aims to honor these stars, pray for individual well-being, and eliminate misfortunes. Various temples across Japan hold Hoshi Matsuri and Star Memorial ceremonies.
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * {{ill|Iwahara Taishin|ja|岩原諦信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwahara Taishin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, *Star Memorial Service and Its Science* (『星供と其科学』), Matsumoto Nisshindō Honten, 1942 (Shōwa 17). * {{ill|Yano Michio|ja|矢野道雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yano Michio|1=Michio Yano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11584444}}, *Esoteric Astrology - Sukuyōdō and Indian Astrology* (『密教占星術 - 宿曜道とインド占星術』), Tokyo Bijutsu, 1986 (Shōwa 61). * {{ill|Hayashi Atsushi|ja|林温|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayashi Atsushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 林ゆたか|1=Yutaka Hayashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11532783}}, *Myōken Bosatsu and the Star Mandala* (『妙見菩薩と星曼荼羅』), Japanese Art No. 377, supervised by {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en|Agency for Cultural Affairs|WD=Q1009519|lt=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}, {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum}}, {{ill|Kyoto National Museum|en|Kyoto National Museum|WD=Q147286|lt=Kyoto National Museum}}, and {{ill|Nara National Museum|en|Nara National Museum|WD=Q147312|lt=Nara National Museum}}, Shibundō, 1997. * Takeda Kazuaki, *A Study of the Star Mandala* (『星曼荼羅の研究』), Hōzōkan, 1995. == See Also == * {{ill|Sukuyōdō|ja|宿曜道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukuyōdō|qq=}} * {{ill|Manjushri Bodhisattva and the Auspicious Sutra of Good and Bad Days|ja|文殊師利菩薩及諸仙所説吉凶時日善悪宿曜経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjushri Bodhisattva and the Auspicious Sutra of Good and Bad Days|1=Sūtra on the Astrology of Auspicious and Inauspicious Days and Good and Evil Spoken by Mañjuśrī Bodhisattva and Celestial Beings|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500028}} * {{ill|Big Dipper|en|Big Dipper|WD=Q10460|lt=Big Dipper}} * {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|WD=Q1340540|lt=Onmyōdō}} * {{ill|Myōken|en|Myōken|WD=Q10960623|lt=Myōken Bosatsu}} * {{ill|Vairocana|en|Vairocana|WD=Q239847|lt=Dainichi Nyorai}} * {{ill|Agon Star Festival|ja|阿含の星まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agon Star Festival|1=Agon Star Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657346}} * {{ill|Goshichinichi Mishuhō|ja|後七日御修法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goshichinichi Mishuhō|1=Goshichinichi Mishiho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124680869}}

In {{ill|Mikkyō|en|Mikkyō|WD=Q336102|lt=Esoteric Buddhism|1=Mikkyō}}, which belongs to the later teachings of the {{ill|Mahayana|ja|大乗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mahayana|1=Q18989147|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18989147}} tradition, deities such as {{ill|Ichiji Kinrin Butchō|ja|一字金輪仏頂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiji Kinrin Butchō|1=Ekākṣaroṣṇīṣacakra|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352518}} (also known as {{ill|Dainichi Kinrin|ja|大日金輪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainichi Kinrin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Shaka Kinrin|ja|釈迦金輪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaka Kinrin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) are enshrined in mandalas like the {{ill|Star Mandala|ja|星曼荼羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star Mandala|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Other examples include the {{ill|Seven-Star Nyoirin Mandala|ja|七星如意輪曼荼羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven-Star Nyoirin Mandala|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} with {{ill|Cintāmaṇicakra|en|Cintāmaṇicakra|WD=Q11446712|lt=Nyoirin Kannon|1=Cintāmaṇicakra}} as the principal deity and the {{ill|Myōken Mandala|ja|妙見曼荼羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Mandala|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} featuring {{ill|Myōken|en|Myōken|WD=Q10960623|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|1=Myōken}}. These mandalas are central to rituals and ceremonies that honor the year's guiding star (当年星, tōnenjō)<ref>"Tōnenjō" is also referred to as "Tōnen Zokusei" (当年属星).</ref> and the natal star (本命星, honmyōjō).<ref>Although the term "honmyōsei" is also used in {{ill|Flying Star Feng Shui|en|Flying Star Feng Shui|WD=Q9384721|lt=Nine Star Ki|1=Flying Star Feng Shui}}, in this context it refers to stars from the {{ill|Big Dipper|en|Big Dipper|WD=Q10460|lt=Big Dipper|1=Big Dipper}} and the {{ill|Nine Luminaries|ja|九曜星|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nine Luminaries|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九曜|1=Navagraha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1078632}}, rather than the nine stars of Ki astrology.</ref>
Originally, this ritual was part of the {{ill|Taoism|en|Taoism|WD=Q9598|lt=Taoist|1=Taoism}} Winter Solstice festival and had no relation to {{ill|Indian Buddhism|ja|インドの仏教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Indian Buddhism|1=Buddhism in India|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2348029}}. However, it was later incorporated into Esoteric Buddhism, where it was adapted and reinterpreted within a Buddhist framework.
In Esoteric astrology, as represented by the Star Mandala, both esoteric rituals and celestial astrology form the core principles. One of the nine {{ill|Navagraha|en|Navagraha|WD=Q1078632|lt=Navagraha|1=Navagraha}} stars or the seven stars of the Big Dipper is assigned as a person's natal star (本命星), believed to govern their destiny.
The star that influences a person's fortune for the year is called the "Tōnen Zokusei" (当年属星). The ritual aims to honor these stars, pray for individual well-being, and eliminate misfortunes. Various temples across Japan hold Hoshi Matsuri and Star Memorial ceremonies.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Hoshi Matsuri]][[ja:星まつり]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Hoshi Matsuri]][[ja:星まつり]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Hoshi Matsuri]][[ja:星まつり]]
'''Hoshi Matsuri''' (星祭)
# An alternative name for {{ill|Qixi Festival|en|Qixi Festival|WD=Q13423122|lt=Tanabata|1=Qixi Festival}}. For details, see the corresponding article. # A colloquial term for "Star Memorial Service" (星供養). This article focuses on this topic.
== Overview ==
[[File:Star Mandala (Horyuji).jpg|thumb|Star Mandala ({{ill|Hōryū-ji|en|Hōryū-ji|WD=Q261932|lt=Hōryū-ji|1=Hōryū-ji}} Collection)]]
'''Hoshi Matsuri''' (星まつり or 星祭り) is a {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|WD=Q748|lt=Buddhist|1=Buddhism}} ritual held on occasions such as the Lunar New Year, {{ill|Lichun|en|Lichun|WD=Q1364685|lt=Risshun|1=Lichun}}, and {{ill|Winter solstice|en|Winter solstice|WD=Q207932|lt=Winter Solstice|1=Winter solstice}}.<ref>In Taoism, the ritual was originally conducted on the Winter Solstice and had no connection to Risshun or the Lunar New Year. It was only after being incorporated into Buddhism that it began to be observed on these additional dates.</ref> The purpose of this ritual is to ward off various disasters affecting both the state and individuals. It is also known as "'''Star Memorial Service'''” (星供養, Hoshikuyō), "'''Star Offering'''” (星供, Hoshiku), or the "'''Big Dipper Ritual'''” (北斗法, Hokutohō).
In {{ill|Mikkyō|en|Mikkyō|WD=Q336102|lt=Esoteric Buddhism|1=Mikkyō}}, which belongs to the later teachings of the {{ill|Mahayana|ja|大乗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mahayana|1=Q18989147|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18989147}} tradition, deities such as {{ill|Ichiji Kinrin Butchō|ja|一字金輪仏頂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiji Kinrin Butchō|1=Ekākṣaroṣṇīṣacakra|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352518}} (also known as {{ill|Dainichi Kinrin|ja|大日金輪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainichi Kinrin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Shaka Kinrin|ja|釈迦金輪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaka Kinrin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) are enshrined in mandalas like the {{ill|Star Mandala|ja|星曼荼羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star Mandala|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Other examples include the {{ill|Seven-Star Nyoirin Mandala|ja|七星如意輪曼荼羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven-Star Nyoirin Mandala|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} with {{ill|Cintāmaṇicakra|en|Cintāmaṇicakra|WD=Q11446712|lt=Nyoirin Kannon|1=Cintāmaṇicakra}} as the principal deity and the {{ill|Myōken Mandala|ja|妙見曼荼羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Mandala|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} featuring {{ill|Myōken|en|Myōken|WD=Q10960623|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|1=Myōken}}. These mandalas are central to rituals and ceremonies that honor the year's guiding star (当年星, tōnenjō)<ref>"Tōnenjō" is also referred to as "Tōnen Zokusei" (当年属星).</ref> and the natal star (本命星, honmyōjō).<ref>Although the term "honmyōsei" is also used in {{ill|Flying Star Feng Shui|en|Flying Star Feng Shui|WD=Q9384721|lt=Nine Star Ki|1=Flying Star Feng Shui}}, in this context it refers to stars from the {{ill|Big Dipper|en|Big Dipper|WD=Q10460|lt=Big Dipper|1=Big Dipper}} and the {{ill|Nine Luminaries|ja|九曜星|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nine Luminaries|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九曜|1=Navagraha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1078632}}, rather than the nine stars of Ki astrology.</ref>
Originally, this ritual was part of the {{ill|Taoism|en|Taoism|WD=Q9598|lt=Taoist|1=Taoism}} Winter Solstice festival and had no relation to {{ill|Indian Buddhism|ja|インドの仏教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Indian Buddhism|1=Buddhism in India|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2348029}}. However, it was later incorporated into Esoteric Buddhism, where it was adapted and reinterpreted within a Buddhist framework.
In Esoteric astrology, as represented by the Star Mandala, both esoteric rituals and celestial astrology form the core principles. One of the nine {{ill|Navagraha|en|Navagraha|WD=Q1078632|lt=Navagraha|1=Navagraha}} stars or the seven stars of the Big Dipper is assigned as a person's natal star (本命星), believed to govern their destiny.
The star that influences a person's fortune for the year is called the "Tōnen Zokusei" (当年属星). The ritual aims to honor these stars, pray for individual well-being, and eliminate misfortunes. Various temples across Japan hold Hoshi Matsuri and Star Memorial ceremonies.
